         <script src= "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@3.0.2/dist/chart.min.js" ></script>

        对于新手来说，数据表使用分页布局提供对大量数据的访问。 一旦必要的代码完成，我们应该有类似的东西：

        要编写这个有用的组件，我们需要一个新row 、一张card和一个card header 。 之后，我们将创建一个卡片主体，然后创建一个响应表格的 div 类。

        转到此链接（引导组件）并复制整个 HTML 部分。 在您的table-responsive课程之后粘贴整个内容。 将class=”display”更改为class="table data-table" 。

        此时，我们的仪表板页面包含所有元素（顶部卡片、图表和数据表），但布局尚未针对良好的用户体验进行优化。 以下是更好布局的步骤。

        在图表和数据表部分之后添加br元素

        此操作将增加行之间的空间，以便用户更喜欢每个部分提供的内容。

        让我们把卡片边框弄圆，给charts 、 data table赋予新的colors 。

        应添加到style.css文件中的 CSS 代码：

         /* table styling */ .card , .card-chart , .card-table { border-radius : 10px ; } .card-chart { background : rgb ( 240 , 240 , 240 ); } .chart { background : rgb ( 230 , 230 , 230 ); border-radius : 5px ; } .card-table { background : rgb ( 240 , 240 , 240 ); }

        我们将通过为每个偶数行着色来使我们的表格具有条纹效果：

         /* striped table effect */ tr :nth-child ( even ) { background-color : rgb ( 250 , 250 , 250 ); }

        以上所有部分的完整 CSS 代码可以在 Github 上找到。

        现在，我们有了一个基本的、最小的仪表板。 如何添加暗模式并让用户决定颜色模式。

        此功能旨在让用户选择应用程序呈现容器的方式：使用浅色或深色背景。 为了实现这个目标，我们需要做的第一件事是在我们的 js 文件夹中创建两个文件： dark-mode-switch.js和dark-mode-switch.min.js （开源 JS 库）。 这将使用浏览器的本地存储处理暗模式和亮模式之间的切换。

        下一步是将暗模式控制切换器编码为一个小的侧边栏按钮。

         <li class= "my-4" > <hr class= "dropdown-divider" > </li> <div class= "custom-control custom-switch px-3" > <label class= "custom-control-label" for= "darkSwitch" > <span class= "fw-bold" > <i class= "bi bi-moon me-2" ></i> Dark Mode </span> </label> <input type= "checkbox" class= "custom-control-input checkbox ms-auto" id= "darkSwitch" > </div>

        以及必要的 CSS 代码：

         /* checkbox */ input [ type = checkbox ] { height : 0px ; width : 0px ; visibility : hidden ; } label { cursor : pointer ; }

        当然，我们需要将设置暗模式样式的 CSS 代码包含到index.html文件中：

         <link rel= "stylesheet" href= "css/dark-mode.css" >

        在这一点上，我们现在几乎完成了黑暗模式，您所要做的就是继续分别设置图表和表格的样式。

        谢谢阅读！ 好奇的人可以通过访问以下资源来进一步研究：



原文:

Hello Coders!

This article explains how to code a simple One-page Dashboard Layout in Bootstrap 5, using the theory from a previous article: Bootstrap - Tutorial for Beginners. The source code for the final project can be downloaded from Github and used in commercial projects or simply for eLearning activities. For newcomers, Bootstrap is a leading JS/CSS framework used to code responsive websites and apps.

Thanks for reading!

  • 👉 Bootstrap 5 Dashboard - LIVE Demo
  • 👉 Bootstrap 5 Dashboard - Source Code

This simple Dashboard provides also a dark-mode switcher positioned on the left sidebar (sources available on GH).

✨ Topics

  • 👉 Bootstrap - Short Presentation
  • 👉 How to install and use Bootstrap
  • 👉 Coding the navbar
  • 👉 Coding the sidebar
  • 👉 Main Content: cards, charts, and data tables
  • 👉 Dark Mode
  • 👉 Links & Resources

✨ Bootstrap Short Intro

Bootstrap is a popular JS/CSS Framework for developing responsive and mobile-first websites, that provides HTML, CSS, and JavaScript-based design templates for almost anything, including (but not limited to): typography, forms, buttons, navigation, and other components.

The primary purpose of adding Bootstrap to a project is to apply Bootstrap's choices of color, size, font, and layout to that project. Currently, Bootstrap 5 is the newest version of Bootstrap, with new components, a faster stylesheet, and more responsiveness.

For more information, feel free to access the official website and docs.

✨ How to use Bootstrap

The easier way to use Bootstrap in a new project is to include all mandatory assets from a CDN (Content Delivery Network). Let's create a new directory for our project with an index.html file, where the new HTML code will be added.

<html lang="en">
<head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <title>Bootstrap Landing Page</title>
</head>
<body> <!-- Page Content here --> </body>
</html>

To speed up our development, we can use a modern programming editor like VsCode or Atom, which comes with modules and many features like code completion and highlighting. Below is the download link for the VsCode:

  • 👉 VsCode - download page

Once the VsCode is installed, we can open our project by typing code . in the working directory (where index.html is saved).

✨ Coding the Navbar

Go to this link and copy the first navbar that has the search bar. Let’s change both the navbar-light and bg-light to dark. We will change the search bar to something nicer, for this we’ll use Input Groups.

<div class="input-group"> <input type="text" class="form-control" placeholder="Recipient's username" aria-label="Recipient's username" aria-describedby="button-addon2"> <button class="btn btn-outline-secondary" type="button" id="button-addon2">Button</button>
</div>

To use Bootstrap 5 icons, we will import them using CDN:

<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap-icons@1.8.1/font/bootstrap-icons.css">

Copy and paste this line of code inside your <head> element.

To make the navbar more appealing, we will change the search button with an icon for a nicer layout.

To make this change we need to copy the search icon code and paste it into the button snippet.

We’re going to do the same thing to the Dropdown text. I’m using an icon of a user.

 <i class="bi bi-person-square"></i>

We have to fix the dropdown menu as well because right now it goes offscreen. Add dropdown-menu-end to dropdown-menu class. This fixes the problem.

Let’s make the navbar-brand font bolder by adding fw-bold to the navbar-brand class.

At this point, all we have is a simple navbar. Let's move forward and code the sidebar.

✨ Dashboard Sidebar

Bootstrap provides Offcanvas, a basic sidebar component that can be toggled via JavaScript to appear from any edge of the viewport.

Here is the code for our sidebar:

<div class="offcanvas offcanvas-start side-bar" data-bs-scroll="true" tabindex="-1" id="offcanvas" aria-labelledby="offcanvas"> <div class="offcanvas-header"> <h5 class="offcanvas-title" id="offcanvas">Sample Heading</h5> <button type="button" class="btn-close text-reset" data-bs-dismiss="offcanvas" aria-label="Close"></button> </div> <div class="offcanvas-body"> <p>Sample Paragraph</p> </div>
</div>

For a nice integration with the navbar we need to add some CSS code listed below:

/* sidebar width */
.side-bar
{ width: var(--offcanvas-width);
} /* sidebar links */
.sidebar-link
{ display: flex; align-items: center;
} .sidebar-link .right-icon
{ display: inline-flex; transition: all ease 0.5s;
}

✨ Main Content

After the sidebar, we’re going to create the main element with a class of mt-5 pt-3.

Right now our content inside the main element is overlapping with our sidebar, to fix it we need to style it. Inside our css media tag, create a new line for main.

Add this code inside it: margin-left: var(--offcanvas-width);. I’m going to make our navbar fixed to the top. To do this I need to add fixed-top class to the navbar class:

Back in our index.html file, let’s start adding content to our main class. Add a container-fluid, row and a col-md-12:

The center of the page will contain three rows:

  • 👉 Info cards
  • 👉 Charts - Powered by Chart.js
  • 👉 Data Tables with paginated information.

Let's break down all these containers and code the HTML and CSS for each section.

👉 Main Content - Cards

Each container (cards, charts ..) will have a parent row where all the HTML code lives. Here is the basic card code for the cards section:

<div class="card text-white bg-primary mb-3" style="max-width: 18rem;"> <div class="card-header">Header</div> <div class="card-body"> <h5 class="card-title">Primary card title</h5> <p class="card-text">Some quick example [.. truncated content ..]</p> </div>
</div>

When we use col-md-3, we can fit 4 cards next to each other because Bootstrap has a 12-column grid.

For a colorful layout, we can change the color of the cards by changing the line bg-primary to danger, warning .. etc. Here is the output:

👉 Main Content - Charts

For the charts section, we will use a 3rd party open-source JS library used to display a simple and colorful bar chart. Each chart lives into a card responsive container that uses 6 cells each.

To code this section, here is the HTML for the card:

<div class="card"> <div class="card-header">Charts</div> <div class="card-body"> <canvas class="chart" width="400" height="200"></canvas> </div>
</div>

We have to make a new folder called js. Inside it, create a new file called script.js. Inside this new file, the following code that builds the charts should be added:

const charts = document.querySelectorAll(".chart"); charts.forEach(function (chart) { var ctx = chart.getContext("2d"); var myChart = new Chart(ctx, { type: "bar", data: { labels: ["Red", "Blue", "Yellow", "Green", "Purple", "Orange"], datasets: [ { label: "# of Votes", data: [12, 19, 3, 5, 2, 3], backgroundColor: [ "rgba(255, 99, 132, 0.2)", "rgba(54, 162, 235, 0.2)", "rgba(255, 206, 86, 0.2)", "rgba(75, 192, 192, 0.2)", "rgba(153, 102, 255, 0.2)", "rgba(255, 159, 64, 0.2)", ], borderColor: [ "rgba(255, 99, 132, 1)", "rgba(54, 162, 235, 1)", "rgba(255, 206, 86, 1)", "rgba(75, 192, 192, 1)", "rgba(153, 102, 255, 1)", "rgba(255, 159, 64, 1)", ], borderWidth: 1, }, ], }, options: { scales: { y: { beginAtZero: true, }, }, }, });
});

In human language, the above JavaScript snippet identifies the cards and injects the data that is translated by the Chart.js library into colorful bar charts.

Of course, Chart.js should be added as a runtime dependency in the index.html file (via a distant CDN).

<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@3.0.2/dist/chart.min.js"></script>

👉 Main Content - Data Tables

For newcomers, data tables provide access to a large amount of data using a paginated layout. Once the necessary code is finished, we should have something similar to this:

To code this useful component, we need a new row, a card, and a card header. After that, we will create a card-body and then a table-responsive div class.

Go to this link (Bootstrap Component) and copy the whole HTML section. Paste the whole thing after your table-responsive class. Change the class=”display” to class="table data-table".

👉 Main Content Styling

At this point, our dashboard page has all the elements (top cards, charts, and data tables) but the layout is not yet optimized for a nice user experience. Here are the steps for a nicer layout.

Add br elements after charts and data tables sections

This action will increase the space between the rows so the users enjoy more the content provided by each section.

Let’s make the card borders round and give charts, data table new colors.

The CSS code that should be added to the style.css file:

/* table styling */
.card,
.card-chart,
.card-table
{ border-radius: 10px;
} .card-chart
{ background: rgb(240, 240, 240);
} .chart
{ background: rgb(230, 230, 230); border-radius: 5px;
} .card-table
{ background: rgb(240, 240, 240);
}

We’ll make our table have the striped effect by coloring every even row:

/* striped table effect */
tr:nth-child(even)
{ background-color: rgb(250, 250, 250);
}

The complete CSS code for all the above sections can be found on Github.

Bootstrap 5 Dashboard - CSS Style.

Now, we have a basic, minimal dashboard. How about adding a dark mode and letting users decide the color schema.

✨ Coding the Dark Mode

This feature aims to let the users choose how the app renders the containers: using a light or a dark background. To achieve this goal, the first thing we need to do is create two files in our js folder: dark-mode-switch.js and dark-mode-switch.min.js (open-source JS library). This will handle the switching between dark mode and light mode using the browser's local storage.

The next step is to code the dark mode control switcher as a small sidebar button.

<li class="my-4"> <hr class="dropdown-divider">
</li>
<div class="custom-control custom-switch px-3"> <label class="custom-control-label" for="darkSwitch"> <span class="fw-bold"> <i class="bi bi-moon me-2"></i>Dark Mode </span> </label> <input type="checkbox" class="custom-control-input checkbox ms-auto" id="darkSwitch">
</div>

And the necessary CSS code:

/* checkbox */
input[type=checkbox]
{ height: 0px; width: 0px; visibility: hidden;
} label
{ cursor: pointer;
}

Of course, we need to include the CSS code that styles the dark mode to the index.html file:

<link rel="stylesheet" href="css/dark-mode.css">

The full code for the dark-mode.css is quite short and self-explanatory:

[data-theme="dark"]
{ background: rgb(65, 65, 65); color: #fff;
} [data-theme="dark"] .bg-black { background-color: #fff !important;
} [data-theme="dark"] .bg-dark { background-color: #222 !important;
} [data-theme="dark"] .bg-light { background-color: #222 !important;
} [data-theme="dark"] .bg-white { background-color: #000 !important;
}

At this point, we're pretty much done with the dark mode now, all you gotta do is go on and style the charts and tables separately.

✨ Links & Resources

Thanks for reading! Curious minds can go further with their study by accessing the resources presented below:

Items.Title: A11Y 101：什么是可访问性 | A11Y 101: What is accessibility
Items.Description:
首先，欢迎来到这个关于可访问性的新系列，这是我一直想探索但从未真正探索过的东西。

到目前为止！

在本系列中，我将了解 Web 可访问性、它是什么、它是谁，以及最重要的是，如何让您的网站更易于访问。

什么是可访问性或 A11Y？

让我们从 A11Y 开始，当人们谈论可访问性时，您经常在科技领域听到这个术语。 这是可访问性的缩写（实际上无法访问🤦‍♂️）。

它的含义来自A{11 characters}Y ，完全是对可访问性一词的简单、快速使用。

那么当我们谈论网络时，这种可访问性到底是什么？

当然，每个人浏览我的网站的方式都是一样的，对吧？

但是那些看不到您的网页但仍想阅读您的内容的人呢？
他们的屏幕阅读器是否真的在阅读您的页面？ 或者用户是否对他们所说的内容感到困惑？

或者只能看到某些色调的人呢？ 你甚至可能有一些明亮的颜色完全淘汰它们。

也可能有些人无法使用键盘或鼠标，而不得不以其他方式浏览您的网站。 您是否坚持让他们可以访问您的网站？

这些只是人们在浏览网站时可能遇到的一些障碍。 在后续文章中，我们将更详细地介绍存在哪些障碍以及它们如何影响 Web 使用。

我们为什么要担心这个？

首先，这是正确的做法。 是什么让残障人士比其他人更少浏览您的网站？

此外，在欧洲，符合 WCAG（无障碍测量标准）实际上是法律

但是，如果您（尚未）顺从，请不要过度自责。
大多数网站都无法通过此检查。

请记住，我们都应该选择让所有人都可以访问该网站，而我们实现这一目标的唯一方法就是从我们自己的网站开始。

从简单开始，尝试对您的网站进行基本审核，然后进行细微更改。

请记住：罗马不是一天建成的，因此您可以通过使您的网站每天更易于访问来做很多事情！

我该如何开始？

这正是我的问题。
坦率地说，我什至不知道从哪里开始。 我做了一些研究，但很快就被文章的数量、来源和对应该做什么的不同意见所淹没。

在这个阶段，我感到非常不知所措。 幸运的是，我在该领域有一些非常好的朋友，他们帮助我引导我穿越了无障碍的神奇世界。

在他们的帮助下，我将这个系列放在一起供您学习。

在本系列中，我们将确定可访问性，对其进行描述，看看我们可以在哪些方面改进我们的网站，甚至学习一些最佳实践。

我希望你能跟随这个旅程。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系

马德斯斯托曼 - Feb 4 '21 21 年 2 月 4 日

Spyros Argalias - Spyros Argalias - Feb 2 '21 21 年 2 月 2 日



原文:

First of all, welcome to this new series on accessibility, something I've wanted to explore but never really did.

Until now!

In this series, I'll be learning about web accessibility, what it is, who it is, and most importantly, how you can make your websites more accessible.

What is accessibility or A11Y?

Let's start with A11Y, a term you often hear in tech when people talk about accessibility. It's an abbreviation (that's not really accessible 🤦‍♂️) for accessibility.

Its meaning comes from A{11 characters}Y, all-and-all a simple, quick use of the term for the word accessibility.

So what in the world is this accessibility when we talk about the web?

Surely everyone navigates my websites the same, right?

But what about people who can't see your webpage but still want to read your content?
Is their screen reader actually reading your page? Or does the user get confused by what it's speaking to them?

Or what about people who can only see certain color tones? You might even have some bright colors that completely phase out for them.

There might also be people who can't use a keyboard or mouse and have to navigate your website in other ways. Are you adhering to making your website accessible for them?

These are just some of the disabilities people could have while navigating a website. In a follow-up article, we'll go into a bit more in detail on which disabilities there are and how they affect web usage.

Why should we worry about this?

First of all, it's the right thing to do. What makes people with disabilities less to browse your website than anyone else?

Furthermore, in Europe, it's actually the law to be WCAG compliant (a standard for accessibility measurements)

However, don't beat yourself up too much if you are not (yet) compliant.
Most websites don't make it past this check.

Remember that we should all opt to make the website accessible for all, and the only way we can achieve this is by starting with our own websites.

Start simple, try a basic audit on your website, and make minor changes.

Remember: Rome wasn't built in one day, so you are doing a lot by making your website more accessible day by day!

How can I get started?

That was precisely my question.
Frankly, I had no clue where to even start. I did some research but was quickly overwhelmed by the number of articles, sources, and different opinions on what should be done.

At this stage, I felt very overwhelmed. Luckily I have some really good friends in the field that helped me guide me through the magical world of accessibility.

And together with their help, I'm putting this series together for you to follow.

During this series, we will identify accessibility, describe it, see where we can improve our website, and even learn some best practices.

I hope you'll follow along on this journey.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Mads Stoumann - Feb 4 '21

Spyros Argalias - Feb 2 '21

Items.Title: 介绍 Depot — 一种更快的构建 Docker 镜像的方法 | Introducing Depot — A faster way to build Docker images
Items.Description:
作为应用程序开发人员将我们的应用程序打包到容器中，或者作为负责为组织提供快速、稳定的构建基础设施的平台工程师，我们花费了大量时间构建和部署 Docker 映像。

在此过程中，我们注意到了一些常见的挑战： docker build可能很慢。 优化速度是乏味的，而且很多时候很脆弱。 通常，CI 提供程序不是为容器构建而设计的，而且它们本身通常是缓慢构建的根源。

今天，我们推出了封闭测试版 Depot，以解决所有这些问题以及更多问题。 这是我们自己想要使用的工具，我们也将它构建到您想要的工具中！

为什么 Docker 构建速度很慢？

能够重用以前的工作是 Docker 快速构建的原因。 知道何时能够重用该缓存并管理缓存的实际内容是使事情变慢的原因。

他们使用层的抽象来描述Dockerfile中的行。 如果您的图像的一层在两次构建之间发生更改，则该点之后的整个缓存都将失效。 在该点之后的所有未来层都必须重新构建。

编写理解这一层缓存的 Dockerfile 是一门艺术。 对于幼稚的事情，它们可能非常简单。 但是有可能导致构建时间爆炸的细微错误。 通过这些复杂的细节获得专业知识并不是每个团队中的每个开发人员都需要或想要花费时间的事情。 同时，这些缓慢的构建会影响生产力和开发人员的幸福感。

为缓存重用优化构建只是 Docker 缓存的第一个挑战。

管理大型 Docker 构建缓存是第二个痛苦的挑战。

在开发人员的笔记本电脑上，Docker 缓存可能会占用千兆字节的磁盘空间。 对于每个本地构建，每个队友的笔记本电脑都会重复使用缓存磁盘。 因此，即使您的同事已经构建了昂贵的 Docker 映像，您也必须自己重新构建它才能在您的机器上使用它。

更糟糕的是，当前的 CI 系统限制了您可以拥有的磁盘空间量。 因此，即使您围绕缓存执行了所有 Docker 最佳实践，它甚至可能不适合磁盘。 或者您必须自己管理缓存的保存/加载，通常会否定您会看到的任何性能提升。

介绍 Depot：我们对所有这些问题的解决方案

当我们开始在 Depot 工作时，我们问自己一个问题，如果这一切都是为我处理的呢？

我们构建 Depot 是为了解决多年来使用docker build所感受到的痛苦。 它由几个关键元素组成，当它们组合在一起时，可以更轻松地在 CI 和本地机器上实现一致的快速构建。 直接与 Docker 的所有最佳缓存机制集成：

  • 每台构建机器提供 4 个 CPU、8GB 内存和 50GB 本地 SSD 缓存
  • 构建器运行 BuildKit，这是最现代、最先进的容器构建器引擎
  • 所有配置，尤其是缓存配置，都得到完全管理，您无需付出任何努力
  • 您可以从现有 CI 提供程序或本地计算机将构建路由到 Depot

仓库构建器带有托管的持久 SSD 缓存。 他们立即启动新构建，所有基本和高级 Docker 缓存在几秒钟内就已经加载完毕。 我们支持您对Dockerfile进行的任何开箱即用优化。

我们的构建者队伍配备的 CPU 和磁盘空间比许多通用 CI 提供商提供的要多。 因此缓存和未缓存的构建都可以很快。 我们的一些早期采用者已经看到了11 倍的加速！

构建器运行最新版本的 BuildKit 以支持 Docker、OCI 和任何其他可用的 BuildKit 输出格式。 我们的 CLI 是docker build的直接替代品，因此采用通常可以像在现有 CI 工作流程中切换到depot build一样简单：

 - run: docker build -t ... + run: depot build -t ...

由于depot build在 CI 和您的笔记本电脑上工作，每个人都可以使用相同的缓存！ 如果您的同事已经构建了该慢速映像，您的构建将自动检测到这一点并下载结果。 如果您在打开拉取请求之前已经在您的机器上构建了映像，那么在检查文件哈希后，您的 CI 构建可能几乎是瞬间完成的！

还有一件事...

随着 Arm CPU 的普及，例如 Apple M1、AWS Graviton 和 Azure Ampere，在 Intel CPU 上为 Arm 目标构建 Docker 映像变得越来越困难，反之亦然。 很少有 CI 提供商提供 Arm 运行器。 如果您需要在单个标签中构建支持两种架构的多平台图像，这可能会特别困难。

当您运行depot build时，我们会检测您机器的架构并将您的构建路由到相同架构的构建器。 您可以使用--platform CLI 标志直接将您的构建路由到另一个目标架构。 我们支持使用--platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64构建多平台映像！

您可以在我们的文档中找到有关多平台构建的更多信息。

加入我们的封闭测试版

这是 Depot 的第一次迭代，我们很高兴将它推向世界！ 我们目前处于封闭测试阶段，很高兴能得到我们能得到的所有反馈。 如果您想在此测试阶段试用 Depot，请注册一个帐户或有任何问题联系我们，我们将为您安排！

我们期待为每个人提供更快的构建速度！

下一步是什么

我们已经在开发下一代 Depot 的构建器。 它在容器构建器空间中为上述所有内容添加了全新的东西：依赖感知缓存。

与其仅仅依赖 Docker 的层缓存（其中缓存未命中会使所有未来的层失效），Depot 即将推出的构建器可以了解每个 Dockerfile 步骤之间的实际依赖关系。 无需任何配置或修改。

这提供了更快的构建，因为缓存被更频繁地重用。 但是，至关重要的是，这也意味着可以快速构建非常简单的 Dockerfile。 这意味着您可以跳过通常完成的大部分 Dockerfile 优化并具有类似的构建性能。

在接下来的几个月里会有更多的事情发生。



原文:

We have spent a lot of time building and deploying Docker images, as application developers packaging our applications into containers, or as platform engineers tasked with providing fast, stable build infrastructure for organizations.

Along the way, we have noticed a few common challenges: docker build can be slow. Optimizing for speed is tedious and many times brittle. Generally, CI providers aren't designed for container builds, and often are themselves the source of slow builds.

Today we are launching Depot in closed beta to solve all these problems and many more. Depot is a hosted container build service that provides blazing fast compute and persistent intelligent caching with zero configuration. It's the tool we wanted to use ourselves, and we're building it into the tool you've wanted as well!

Why are Docker builds slow?

Being able to reuse previous work is what makes Docker builds fast. Knowing when you're able to reuse that cache, and managing the actual contents of the cache, are what make things slow.

Docker, and its modern engine BuildKit, have very rudimentary caching. They use the abstraction of layers to describe lines in a Dockerfile. If one layer of your image changes between two builds, the entire cache after that point gets invalidated. All future layers after that point must be built again.

There's a certain art to writing Dockerfiles that understand this layer caching. They can be quite simple for naive things. But there's a potential for subtle mistakes that can cause build times to explode. Gaining the expertise with these intricate details isn't something every developer on every team needs or wants to spend their time on. At the same time, these slow builds can affect productivity and developer happiness.

Optimizing builds for cache reuse is only the first challenge with Docker caching.

Managing large Docker build caches is the second painful challenge.

On a developer's laptop, Docker caches can take up gigabytes of disk space. That cache disk usage is duplicated on every teammate's laptop for every local build. So even if your coworker has already built the expensive Docker image, you have to re-build it yourself from scratch to use it on your machine.

To make matters worse, current CI systems restrict the amount of disk space you can have. So even if you do all the Docker best practices around caching, it may not even fit on disk. Or you have to manage the saving/loading of cache yourself, oftentimes negating any performance boost you would see.

Introducing Depot: Our solution to all these problems

When we started working on Depot, we asked ourselves one question, what if all this was just handled for me?

We built Depot to address the pain we've felt with docker build over the years. Depot is a remote container build service where your builds are executed on our fast machines with persistent disks.

It consists of a few key elements that, when combined, make it easier to achieve consistently fast builds in CI and on local machines. Directly integrating with all of Docker's best caching mechanisms:

  • Each build machine offers 4 CPUs, 8GB of memory, and 50GB of local SSD cache
  • Builders run BuildKit, the most modern, advanced container builder engine
  • All configuration, especially cache configuration, is fully managed with no effort on your part
  • You can route builds to Depot from your existing CI provider or from your local machine

Depot builders come with a managed persistent SSD cache. They instantly launch new builds with all the basic and advanced Docker cache already loaded in mere seconds. We support any optimizations you've made to your Dockerfile out of the box.

Our fleet of builders come with more CPUs and disk space than many generic CI providers offer. So both cached and uncached builds can be quick. Some of our early adopters have seen speedups of 11x!

Depot is fully compatible with the existing ecosystem of container tooling. Builders run the latest version of BuildKit to support Docker, OCI, and any other available BuildKit output formats. Our CLI is a drop-in replacement for docker build, so adoption can often be as simple as switching to depot build in your existing CI workflow:

- run: docker build -t ...
+ run: depot build -t ...

Another cool thing about Depot is that, as a remote build service, time savings from caching is shared. Since depot build works in CI and on your laptop, everyone gets to use the same cache! If your coworker already built that slow image, your build will automatically detect this and just download the result. If you've already built your image on your machine before opening a pull request, your CI build might be almost instantaneous after the file hashes are checked!

One more thing...

With the rise in popularity of Arm CPUs, for instance, Apple M1, AWS Graviton, and Azure Ampere, it's becoming difficult to build Docker images on Intel CPUs for Arm targets, or vice versa. Very few CI providers offer Arm runners at all. And it can be especially difficult if you need to build multi-platform images that support both architectures in a single tag.

Depot supports both Intel (amd64) and Arm (arm64) builds natively, without emulation.

When you run depot build, we detect the architecture of your machine and route your build to a builder of the same architecture. You can directly route your build to another target architecture with the --platform CLI flag. And we support multi-platform images built in a single pass with --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64!

You can find more info about multi-platform builds in our documentation.

Join our closed beta

This is the first iteration of Depot, and we're excited to launch it into the world! We are currently in closed beta and are very excited to get all the feedback we can get. If you want to give Depot a try during this beta phase, please sign up for an account or contact us with any questions and we will set you up!

We look forward to making builds faster for everyone!

What's next

We are already working on the next generation of Depot's builder. It adds to all the above something entirely new in the container builder space: dependency-aware caching.

Rather than relying solely on Docker's layer caching, where cache misses invalidate all future layers, Depot's upcoming builder can understand the actual dependencies between each Dockerfile step. Without any configuration or modification.

This provides faster builds since caches are reused more often. But, critically, it also means vastly simpler Dockerfiles build quickly. Meaning you can skip much of the Dockerfile optimization typically done and have similar build performance.

More to come on this in the following months.
Items.Author: kylegalbraith

Items.Title: ReactPlay 简介 - 学习、创建、分享 ReactJS 项目 | Introducing ReactPlay - Learn, Create, Share ReactJS projects
Items.Description:
如果我告诉你 React（又名 ReactJS）是 Web 开发中领先的用户界面技术（好吧，库）之一，你会相信吗？ 你们中的大多数人都会的。 对于你们其他人，让我提出一些统计数据。

根据 Stack Overflow Trends， React是自 2014 年以来最新兴的基于 JavaScript 的用户界面技术。这一趋势完全基于使用情况。


资料来源： 堆栈溢出趋势

资料来源： 2021 年堆栈溢出调查

在过去的几年里，我们看到了像Gatsby这样的流行 Web 框架， Next.js选择 ReactJS 作为构建的基础技术。 随着 Web3 的蓬勃发展，React 也是领跑者。 尽管周围还有其他出色的框架和库，但对 React 的需求是常青的，并且到目前为止已得到证明。

然而，我们似乎错过了一些东西。

需求

在 2022 年初，我发布了一份经过修订的完整路线图，以在 freeCodeCamp 上学习 ReactJS 。 令我惊讶的是，我在 Twitter DM 上收到了一些很棒的问题。 这里有几个，

  • 路线图很棒，但是作为初学者应该构建什么？
  • 我们如何知道我们正在使用 React 进行正确的编码？
  • 我可以研究一个项目并构建一些受其启发的东西吗？
  • 有人可以查看我的代码并给我反馈吗？

这些都不是额外的要求。 如果你正在学习一些东西，你需要练习。 要练习，您需要构建项目。 要构建项目，您需要知道构建什么以及如何构建。 一旦你弄清楚了，接下来就是从反馈和评论中学习和超越。 最后，您需要告诉世界您已经努力学习并热情地赢得了它。 所以你需要对你所取得的成就进行社交。

当这些想法在我小小的大脑中多向波动时，我想，为什么不建立一个社区驱动的 ReactJS 学习平台呢？ 在那一刻， ReactPlay的想法开始在我脑海中萌芽。

什么是 ReactPlay？

您可以从浏览许多基于 ReactJS 的项目开始，例如初学者、中级和高级。 它允许您从与项目相关的代码、解释、文章和视频中学习，并激发您创建一个项目。

我们在 ReactPlay 中称每个项目为一个play 。 当您通过几个简单的引导步骤创建一个游戏时，它会经过经验丰富的 ReactJS 开发人员的一些认真的代码审查。 在代码审查过程中，您将了解使用 React 正确执行代码的几个方面，“在 React 中思考”。 在成功的代码审查之后，您的游戏被接受并成为 ReactPlay 的一部分，以激励社区中的许多其他人。 因此，它是一个学习、创建和分享有关 React 的链。

这里有一些重要的链接供您查看，

让我们了解如何！

您可以在播放列表页面上找到它们。 交互式搜索和过滤功能使您可以找到您感兴趣的戏剧并了解详细信息。

每个项目都有详细的标题、描述、创建者以及与之交互的游乐场。 交互性有助于您了解项目的目的，并且在任何时候，您都可以深入到源代码中学习。

如果游戏创建者将项目与博客文章或 YouTube 视频链接，它可以帮助您进一步阅读和了解它。 有疑问或反馈？ 您可以使用Comments功能立即开始与游戏创作者的对话。

请从此处阅读有关此实现的更多信息：使用 React 和 GitHub 讨论的评论系统。

总的来说，向他人学习的机会只会加速你的学习并激励你做更多的事情。 但是，等等，谁批准了这些项目？ 如何创建一个？

你可以用你选择的任何想法来创造戏剧。 如果您的想法用完了，请查看想法页面以选择并创建。 如果您不确定或有任何疑问，请开始讨论，我们将确保您开始。

一旦您确定了游戏创意，请按照以下步骤开始。

  • 首先，您需要分叉react-play 存储库。 您可以通过单击 repo 右上角的Fork按钮来执行此操作。 如果您不熟悉分叉，请观看此YouTube 指南。

  • 分叉后，您可以通过单击分叉存储库右上角的Clone or Download按钮来克隆存储库。

  • 接下来，通过在react-play存储库中运行以下命令来安装依赖项：

 npm install # Or yarm install
  • 使用以下命令以开发模式启动应用程序：
 npm start # Or yarn start

它以开发模式运行应用程序。 打开http://localhost:3000在浏览器中查看。

  • 在项目文件夹中打开另一个命令提示符并运行以下命令
 yarn create-play # or npm run create-play

它会询问一些关于您的Play的问题，然后执行创建该游戏所需的步骤。 下面的屏幕截图显示了命令的输出。 因此，您可以将它们中的任何一种用作您的游戏的基本语言。

它会自动帮助创建者创建脚手架、设置导入和初始代码结构。 创建者可以将项目导入他们喜欢的代码编辑器（如 VS Code）并开始实现播放逻辑。 这很简单。 试试看。

完成 Play 编码后，您可以将其提交以供审核。 提交 Play 以供审核是一个两步过程。

  • 使用您的更改在react-play存储库上创建一个拉取请求。
  • 每周花一些时间来处理审阅意见。

一旦拉取请求被批准和合并，我们将通知您并将您添加为react-play项目的贡献者。 您的演奏将出现在播放列表页面上，供其他人查看和学习。

这意味着您可以使用唯一的 URL 直接访问戏剧。 您可以在社交媒体平台上共享相同的 URL 以供其他人访问。 该剧以其封面图片、标题和元描述出现。 这是一个例子，

新播放提醒📢叫做​​ Keeper。

想记下并保存在某个地方📝看看😃

此外，您可以在 Facebook、Twitter、LinkedIn 和 Reddit 上分享该剧，只需单击“分享”模式。

所以，让全世界都知道你的工作和成就。

你可以如何贡献？

想为 ReactPlay 做贡献吗？ 这很容易。 只需从创建剧本开始。 它将帮助你练习你所学到的关于 ReactJS 的一切。 同时，您还将为一个开源项目做出贡献。 这是一个双赢。

但是，如果您不是 React 开发人员并且仍想为项目做出贡献怎么办？ 没问题，你可以。 这里是如何，

我可以为 ReactPlay 做贡献吗？🤩：当然可以。 开源不仅仅是关于代码。 您可以，- 改进文档- 提出想法- 测试应用程序- 改进 UX- 参与讨论。

前往我们的 GitHub 存储库。 社区随着学习、分享和贡献而成长。 自 2022 年 4 月开始一个月以来，ReactPlay 项目团队很自豪拥有 15 多个贡献者。你们是摇滚明星 🌟 🌟🌟。


当我们有更多的贡献者时，我希望每周更改上面的图片🤩

我们非常认真地想通过想法、功能和参与来进一步发展它。 我们目前正在开发这些排名前 5 位的优先功能，

  • 集成身份验证系统。
  • 将 Play 数据保存在 Data Store 中。
  • 喜欢戏剧的能力。
  • 使用自动算法显示热门游戏。
  • 创建排行榜以鼓励 Play 创作者。

我们还将改变游戏在代码级别的架构方式。 许多令人兴奋的作品即将到来，您可以在其中发挥重要作用。

  • 加入我们，在我们的讨论板上构思。
  • 随意为错误或功能请求创建问题。
  • 帮助我们构建我们的前 5 个功能。
  • 创作戏剧，并写下它们。
  • 加入代码审查委员会。

目前为止就这样了。 让我们一起为 ReactJS 开发者学习、分享和构建 ReactPlay 社区。

我分享我的知识，

  • 让我们连接，



原文:

If I tell you React(aka ReactJS) is one of the leading user interface technology(ok, library) in web development, would you believe it? Most of you will. For the rest of you, let me put forward some stats.

As per the Stack Overflow Trends, React is the most emerging JavaScript-based user interface technology since 2014. This trend is entirely based on usage.


Source: Stack Overflow Trends

React was among the most used web frameworks used in 2021 and continues.


Source: Stack overflow survey 2021

In the last few years, we have seen popular web frameworks like Gatsby, and Next.js choose ReactJS as the base technology to build upon. With the Web3 boom as well, React is the front runner. While there are other remarkable frameworks and libraries around, the demand for React is evergreen, and it has been proven so far.

The React Developer Communities are very active in learning and sharing on various social media platforms like Twitter, LinkedIn, Showwcase, etc. There are many tutorials, YouTube videos, and courses to help many beginners get started with ReactJS. However, we seem to miss something.

The Need

In early 2022, I published a revised, full-fledge roadmap to learn ReactJS on freeCodeCamp. To my surprise, I have received some great questions on Twitter DMs. Here are a few,

  • The roadmap is great, but what to build as a beginner?
  • How do we know we are doing the right coding with React?
  • Can I look into a project and build something inspired by it?
  • Can someone look into my code and give me feedback?

None of these was an extra ask. If you are learning something, you need to practice. To practice, you need to build projects. To build projects, you need to know what to build and how. Once you figure it out, the next thing is to learn and excel from the feedback and reviews. Finally, you need to tell the world that you have learned it hard and earned it passionately. So you need to socialize on what you have accomplished.

As these thoughts were taking multidirectional swings inside my tiny brain, I thought, why not build a platform for community-driven learning of ReactJS? At that moment, the idea of ReactPlay started seeding in my mind.

What is ReactPlay?

ReactPlay is an Opensource platform to learn, create and share ReactJS projects with the developer community. You can start by browsing through many ReactJS based projects like beginners, intermediate, and advanced. It allows you to learn from the code, explanation, articles, and videos related to a project and inspire you to create one.

We call each project a play in ReactPlay. When you create a play with a few simple guided steps, it goes through some serious code reviews by experienced ReactJS developers. In the code review process, you learn several aspects of doing it right with React, "Thinking in React". After a successful code review, your play gets accepted and be part of ReactPlay to inspire many others in the community. So it is a chain to learn, create, and share all about React.

Here are some important links for you to look into,

How ReactPlay Can Help React Developers to Grow?

The motto of the ReactPlay platform is to help developers to Learn, Create, and Socialize. Let's understand how!

👩‍🎓 Learn from Existing Plays

The ReactPlay web application lists the React projects of different experience levels. You can find them on the playlist page. The interactive search and filter feature allows you to find the plays of your interest and get into the details.

Each project is detailed with a title, description, who created it, and a playground to interact with it. The interactive nature helps you understand the purpose of the project, and at any point in time, you can dive into the source code to learn from it.

If a play creator links the project with a blog article or YouTube video, it helps you further read and learn about it. Got a doubt or feedback? You can instantly start a conversation with the play creator using the Comments feature.

GitHub's Discussions feature powers the comments feature. Please read more about this implementation from here: Comment system using React and GitHub Discussions.

Overall, the opportunity to learn from others will only accelerate your learning and inspire you to do more. But, hang on, who approved these projects? How to create one?

🏆 Create a Play

ReactPlay encourages you to create plays(aka projects) using a few simple steps. You can create plays with any ideas of your choice. If you run out of an idea, please take a look at the ideas page to pick one and create. If you are unsure or have any doubts, start a discussion, and we will make sure you get started.

Once you have settled on the play idea, please follow these steps to get started.

  • First, you need to fork the react-play repo. You can do this by clicking the Fork button on the top right corner of the repo. If you are new to forking, please watch this YouTube Guide.

  • Once forked, you can clone the repo by clicking the Clone or Download button on the top right corner of the forked repo.

  • Next, install the dependencies by running the following command in the react-play repo:

 npm install # Or yarm install
  • Use the following command to start the app in the development mode: 
 npm start # Or
    yarn start

It runs the app in the development mode. Open http://localhost:3000 to view it in your browser.

  • Open another command prompt in your project folder and run the following command 
 yarn create-play # or
    npm run create-play

It will ask a few questions about your Play and then perform the required steps to create the play. The screenshot below shows the output of the command. ReactPlay is equipped with both JavaScript and TypeScript. So you can use either of them as the base language for your play.

ReactPlay uses PlopJS to automate the play creation. It automatically helps create the scaffolding, setting imports, and initial code structure for the creators. The creator can import the project into their favourite code editor like VS Code and start implementing the play logic. It's simple. Give it a try.

After you are done with coding for your Play, you can submit it for review. Submitting a Play for review is a two-step process.

  • Create a Pull Request on the react-play repository with your changes.
  • Dedicate some time in a week to take care of the review comments.

Once the Pull Request is approved and merged, we will notify you and add you as a contributor to the react-play project. Your play will be available on the playlist page for others to take a look and learn from it.

🚀 Socialize Your Play

ReactPlay supports deep linking. It means you can access a play directly with a unique URL. You can share the same URL on social media platforms for others to access it. The play appears with its cover image, title, and meta description. Here is an example,

New Play alert 📢It’s called Keeper.

🖌️ Created by @Shivamkatare_27

Want to take note and keep it somewhere 📝Have a look 😃

reactplay.io/plays/keeper

Additionally, you can share the play on Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, and Reddit with a single click from the Share modal.

So, let the whole world know about your work and achievements.

How Can You Contribute?

Want to contribute to ReactPlay? It is easy. Just get started by creating a play. It will help you to practice everything that you learn about ReactJS. At the same time, you will also contribute to an Opensource project. It's a Win-Win.

But what if you are not a React developer and still want to contribute to the project? No problem, you can. Here is how,

🤯: I don't know React. Can I contribute to ReactPlay?🤩: Absolutely. Opensource is NOT only about the code. You can,- Improve the doc- Suggest Ideas- Test the app- Improve UX- Take part in discussions.

Head over to our GitHub repo. The 🔗 is in comment below

A community grows with learning, sharing, and contributions. ReactPlay project team is proud to have 15+ contributors since we started a month back in April 2022. You folks are rockstars 🌟 🌟🌟.


I wish to change the above image every week as we get more contributors 🤩

The Future of ReactPlay

The ReactPlay project is still at its early stage and running several proofs-of-concept. We are damn serious about growing it further with ideas, features, and engagements. We are working on these top-5 priority features currently,

  • Integrating an Authentication System.
  • Persisting the Play data in a Data Store.
  • Ability to Like Plays.
  • Show Trending Plays with an Automated Algorithm.
  • Create a Leader Board to Encourage Play Creators.

We will also make changes in the way plays are architected at the code level. Lots of exciting pieces to come, and you can play a significant role in them.

  • Join us in ideating on our Discussion Board.
  • Feel free to create an issue for a bug or feature request.
  • Helps us build our top-5 features.
  • Create plays, and write about them.
  • Join the Code Review board.

That's all for now. Let's learn, share, and build the ReactPlay community together for the ReactJS Developers.

I share my knowledge on,

  • 🌐 Web Development(JavaScript, ReactJS, Next.js, Node.js, so on...)
  • 🛡️ Web Security
  • 💼 Career Development
  • 🌱 Opensource
  • ✍️ Content Creation

Let's connect,
Items.Author: atapas

Items.Title: 如何在 Lua 中构建一个基本的 REST API - Milua 微框架 | How to build a basic REST API in Lua - Milua micro framework
Items.Description:
米格尔MJ

发表于我最近开始了一个新项目，我想为此构建一个 REST API。 以下是我考虑过的替代方案以及我拒绝它们的原因：

  • 使用我更熟悉的框架，使用不同的语言，例如Flask for Python 。 我的项目已经需要 Lua 并使用了许多不同的技术，所以我更愿意保持简单，而不是在列表中添加全新的语言和框架。
  • 使用 Lua 中的现有框架，例如Lapis 。 这里的问题是 Lapis 依赖于第三方软件，如 Nginx，因此依赖关系列表会不断增长。 在一个更繁重的项目中这不会是一个问题，但我在这里需要的 API 真的很小，所以我会考虑过度杀戮而不是单一的技术。

封面图片由aloiswohlfahrt在Pixabay上发布

米卢阿

我清楚地感觉到我真正想要的是一个纯粹的 Lua 解决方案。 经过一番研究，我没有找到任何事实上的标准（我可能错了），但我确实找到了duarnimator的http库，它提供了一组出色的功能，可以在几行代码中启动一个简单的服务器。

受到使用 Flask 经验的启发，我制作了Milua

安装

我了解了 lua 包管理器luarocks的工作原理并发布了第一个版本，因此您只能使用它的依赖项安装它：

例子

之后，您可以尝试存储库中的示例。

 local app = require "milua" -- Basic example app . add_handler ( "GET" , "/" , function () return "<h1>Welcome to the handsome server!</h1>" end ) -- Example capturing a path variable app . add_handler ( "GET" , "/user/..." , function ( captures , query , headers ) local username = captures [ 1 ] local times = query . times or 1 return "The user " .. username .. " is" .. ( " very" ): rep ( times ) .. " handsome" end ) app . start ()

启动它只需要您在安装 Milua 后执行脚本。 然后，您可以使用curl对其进行测试。

 $ curl localhost:8800/ <h1>Welcome to the handsome server!</h1> $ curl localhost:8800/user/foo The user foo is very handsome $ curl localhost:8800/user/foo?times=3 The user foo is very very very handsome

特征

目前，在我编写的 0.1 版本中， milua模块仅提供两个功能：

  • 事实上，这个模式只是一个带有一些语法糖的常规 Lua 模式，所以你可以在你的路径中捕获任何你喜欢的东西，并且可以根据你的需要进行具体化： /([0-9]+)将捕获一个数字的路径价值; 并且必须返回以下值：
    • 响应的正文。
    • 结论

      仍然有一些修改的空间可以将复杂性保持在同一水平，例如添加错误处理程序（例如，允许404.html页面）或指定静态文件的目录。 例如，它没有模板功能（可能在将来），但您可以要求任何您的偏好并在您的应用程序中使用它。 但是现在它服务于它的目的，并且由于它的最小性质，它与你想要扔给它的任何东西都兼容。

      我仍在学习 Lua 及其工具，将来可能会写更多关于这种语言的文章。 你对 Lua 有什么体验？ 您以前用于 Web 开发吗？ 我很乐意阅读您的任何评论！

      推荐阅读

      你可以在推特上关注我！ 🐦 🐦



原文:

MiguelMJ

Posted on May 17

I recently started a new project, for which I wanted to build a REST API. Here are the alternatives I considered and why I rejected them:

  • Using a framework I'm more familiar with, in a different language, like Flask for Python. My project already required Lua and used a bunch of different technologies, so I'd prefer to keep it simple and not add a whole new language and framework to the list.
  • Using an existing framework in Lua, like Lapis. The problem here was that Lapis relies on third-party software, like Nginx, so the dependencies list would keep growing. This wouldn't be a problem in a more heavy project, but the API I need here is really small, so I would consider overkill anything more than a single technology for it.

Cover image by aloiswohlfahrt from Pixabay

Milua

I felt clearly that what I really wanted was a pure Lua solution. After a bit of research I didn't find any de-facto standard (I might be wrong), but I did find a http library by duarnimator that provided an excellent set of functionalities to launch a simple server in a few lines.

So inspired by the experience of using Flask I made Milua

Installation

I learned how the luarocks, a lua package manager, works and published the first version, so you can install it with it's dependencies only with:

Examples

Right after, you can try the example that comes in the repository.

local app = require "milua" -- Basic example
app.add_handler( "GET", "/", function() return "<h1>Welcome to the handsome server!</h1>" end
) -- Example capturing a path variable
app.add_handler( "GET", "/user/...", function (captures, query, headers) local username = captures[1] local times = query.times or 1 return "The user " .. username .. " is" .. (" very"):rep(times) .. " handsome" end
) app.start()

Launching this would just require you to execute the script after installing Milua. Then, you can test it with curl.

$ curl localhost:8800/
<h1>Welcome to the handsome server!</h1> 

$ curl localhost:8800/user/foo
The user foo is very handsome

$ curl localhost:8800/user/foo?times=3
The user foo is very very very handsome

Features

For now, version 0.1 as I write, the milua module only offers two functions:

  • add_handler(method, pattern, handler) to associate a method and a path to a handler.
    • The handler function must accept the following arguments:
      • captures: An array with the variables fields of the path, specified with three dots (...) in the pattern string. In fact, this pattern is just a regular Lua pattern with some syntactic sugar, so you can capture anything you like in your path and be as specific as you want: /([0-9]+) would capture a path to a numeric value; /admin_... would capture the destination without the prefix admin_.
      • query: A table with the key-value pairs of the query in the URL (the options that come after the ?).
      • headers: The headers of the HTTP request.
      • body: The body of the HTTP request.
    • and must return the following values:
      • The body of the repsonse.
      • (Optional) A table with the headers of the response.
  • start(config) where config contains the host and the port to run the application.

Conclusion

There is still room for a couple of modifications that would keep the complexity at the same level, like adding error handlers (to allow a 404.html page, for example) or specifying directories for static files. For example, it has no templating functionalities (maybe in a future), but you can require any of your preference and use it in your app. But for now it servers its purpose and it's compatible with anything you want to throw at it, thanks to its minimal nature.

I'm still learning about Lua and its tools and might write more about this language in the future. What's your experience with Lua? Have you used before for web development? I'll be happy to read any comment you have!

Recommended reading

You can follow me on Twitter! 🐦
Items.Author: miguelmj

Items.Title: 使用 OpenTelemetry 和 SigNoz 跟踪 Ruby 应用程序💎 | Tracing a Ruby application with OpenTelemetry and SigNoz 💎
Items.Description:
有必要监视 Ruby 应用程序的性能问题。 在今天的云原生和基于微服务的架构中，工程团队很难排查性能问题。

跟踪您的应用程序可以提供解决性能问题所需的急需上下文。 什么是分布式跟踪？
分布式跟踪是一种在用户请求跨分布式系统组件传输时对其进行整体跟踪的方法。

在为用户提供所需的服务之前，单个用户请求可能会经过数百个服务。 使用 OpenTelemetry 客户端库，您可以从 Ruby 应用程序收集跟踪数据，以监控用户请求在服务之间的执行情况。

然后可以使用像SigNoz这样的可观察性工具来可视化跟踪数据。 在我们演示如何为 Ruby 应用程序实现 OpenTelemetry 客户端库之前，让我们简要概述一下 OpenTelmetry。

什么是 OpenTelemetry？

它旨在使遥测数据（日志、指标和跟踪）成为云原生软件应用程序的内置功能。

然后将遥测数据发送到可观察性工具进行存储和可视化。



原文:

Ruby on Rails is a popular MVC framework for creating web applications. It is necessary to monitor your Ruby applications for performance issues. In today’s cloud-native and microservices-based architecture, it is difficult for engineering teams to troubleshoot performance issues.

Tracing your application can give the much needed context required to troubleshoot performance issues. OpenTelemetry is an open-source project that can help you to set up an observability framework for your cloud-native applications.

What is Distributed Tracing?
Distributed tracing is a method to track user requests in their entirety as it travels across components of a distributed system.

A single user request might go through hundreds of services before serving the user what they need. Using OpenTelemetry client libraries, you can collect tracing data from your Ruby applications to monitor how user requests are performing across services.

The tracing data can then be visualized using an observability tool like SigNoz. Before we demonstrate how to implement the OpenTelemetry client libraries for a Ruby application, let’s have a brief overview of OpenTelmetry.

What is OpenTelemetry?

OpenTelemetry is an open-source vendor-agnostic set of tools, APIs, and SDKs used to instrument applications to create and manage telemetry data(logs, metrics, and traces). It aims to make telemetry data(logs, metrics, and traces) a built-in feature of cloud-native software applications.

The telemetry data is then sent to an observability tool for storage and visualization.

OpenTelemetry is the bedrock for setting up an observability framework. It also provides you the freedom to choose a backend analysis tool of your choice.

OpenTelemetry and SigNoz

In this article, we will use SigNoz as our backend analysis tool. SigNoz is a full-stack open-source APM tool that can be used for storing and visualizing the telemetry data collected with OpenTelemetry. It is built natively on OpenTelemetry and works on the OTLP data formats.

SigNoz provides query and visualization capabilities for the end-user and comes with out-of-box charts for application metrics and traces.

Now let’s get down to how to implement OpenTelemetry Ruby libraries and then visualize the collected data in SigNoz.

Install SigNoz

First, you need to install SigNoz so that OpenTelemetry can send the data to it.

SigNoz can be installed on macOS or Linux computers in just three steps by using a simple install script.

The install script automatically installs Docker Engine on Linux. However, on macOS, you must manually install Docker Engine before running the install script.

git clone -b main https://github.com/SigNoz/signoz.git
cd signoz/deploy/
./install.sh

You can visit our documentation for instructions on how to install SigNoz using Docker Swarm and Helm Charts.

When you are done installing SigNoz, you can access the UI at http://localhost:3301

Instrumenting a Ruby on Rails application with OpenTelemetry

Step 1: Install dependencies

Install dependencies related to OpenTelemetry SDK and exporter using gem.

gem install opentelemetry-sdk
gem install opentelemetry-exporter-otlp
gem install opentelemetry-instrumentation-all

Include the required packages into your gemfile.

gem 'opentelemetry-sdk'
gem 'opentelemetry-exporter-otlp'
gem 'opentelemetry-instrumentation-all'

Run the bundle install command:

Step 2: Initialize the OpenTelemetry SDK

Initialize the otel sdk by adding below lines to config/environment.rb of your Ruby on Rails application.

require 'opentelemetry/sdk'
require_relative 'application' OpenTelemetry::SDK.configure do |c| c.use_all
end Rails.application.initialize!

Step 3: Running your Ruby application

Now we have to set the following environment variables to export the collected telemetry data for storage and visualization.

  • OTEL_EXPORTER : It is the format of exported data. Since SigNoz natively supports otlp so it should be set as otlp

  • OTEL_SERVICE_NAME : Name of service(anything you want)

  • OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT : Specify the endpoint of OTEL collector in format http://IP_OF_SIGNOZ:4318. The OTEL collector comes bundled with SigNoz installation. Since, we installed SigNoz on our local machine, the endpoint is http://localhost:4318.

  • OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES : Pass custom attributes like application name with OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES=application=yourAppName

Using the above mentioned environment variables, run the application:

OTEL_EXPORTER=otlp OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=yourSampleRailsApp OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4318 OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES=application=sparkapp rails server

Monitor your Ruby on Rails application with Signoz

After following above steps, you can now monitor your Rails app with SigNoz. You can use this sample Ruby on Rails app that was instrumented using above steps to see how the collected data is visualized with SigNoz dashboard.

To run the sample app use below commands

  • Install dependencies: bundle install
  • Migrate database: rails db:migrate
  • Run the app with env variables: 
OTEL_EXPORTER=otlp OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=sampleRailsApp OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4318 OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES=application=sparkapp rails server rails server

Now the app should be running at http://localhost:3000/

Play around with the app to generate some demo monitoring data, which will automatically be exported to the SigNoz Otel collector.

Now navigate to http://localhost:3301/application (needs signup) to analyse the telemetry data of Rails app.

You can analyze your tracing data with powerful filters using the Traces tab on SigNoz dashboard.

Using Flamegraphs and Gantt charts, you can see a complete breakdown of your request.

Conclusion

Using OpenTelemetry libraries, you can instrument your Ruby applications for setting up observability. You can then use an open-source APM tool like SigNoz to monitor the collected data.

OpenTelemetry is the future for setting up observability for cloud-native apps. It is backed by a huge community and covers a wide variety of technology and frameworks. Using OpenTelemetry, engineering teams can instrument polyglot and distributed applications with peace of mind.

SigNoz is an open-source observability tool that comes with a SaaS-like experience. You can try out SigNoz by visiting its GitHub repo 👇

If you have any questions or need any help in setting things up, join our slack community and ping us in #support channel.

Further Reading

OpenTelemetry Collector - The complete guide

SigNoz - an open-source alternative to DataDog
Items.Author: ankit01oss

Items.Title: 我进行了第一次技术演讲：事情是这样的。 | I gave my first tech talk: here is how it went.
Items.Description:
上周，我有幸在 Women Who Code London Object Oriented Programming 轨道上谈论了 SOLID 原则。

好玩。 这也极具挑战性。

首先，在此之前，我只在公司内部进行过内部技术演示。 进行面向外部的技术演讲绝对是令人伤脑筋的。 这种 SOLID 的东西相当复杂，焦虑是真实的。 公开演讲对我们大多数人来说都是可怕的，尽管我在处理围绕它的焦虑方面肯定会越来越好。

其次，作为一名自学成才的工程师，我不断地与自我怀疑和冒名顶替综合症作斗争。 如果我的代码示例中有错误怎么办？ 如果我扼杀了一个解释而有人误解了一个原则怎么办？ 如果我收到一个我不知道答案的问题怎么办？ 这些担忧始终存在，尽管我试图忽略它们并尽我所能做好准备。 如果我尽我所能提供最好的内容，那么我可以安全地知道我已经完成了我可以控制的所有事情，然后放开其他所有事情。 至少这是我的希望。

第三，生活发生了。 当我举办这个网络研讨会时，我碰巧和一只可爱的小猫坐在一起，当我开始谈论 SOLID 时它显然变得过度兴奋（谁能怪他，对吧？），并决定跳到我的键盘上咬我的腿是分享乐趣的方式。 我摆出我能做到的最好的扑克脸，但我仍然感到沮丧和有点生气。 我花了数周时间准备一个完美的演讲，现在我不得不处理一些让我失望的意外和烦人的事情。

尽管如此，演讲还是很顺利的，参与者很享受，我的解释清晰而有节奏，我也玩得很开心。

这一切都让我思考。 那些把自己放在网上、教我们或娱乐我们的人的生活中发生了很多事情，这些事情并不总是可见的，但仍然对他们构成挑战。 我们从未见过这些，但我们仍然在评判他们，并花更多的时间批评他们的失败，而不是赞扬他们的成功。

经历这样的经历教会了我对他人的同理心以及他们正在经历的事情，同时也让我学会了韧性和对自己更友善。

公开演讲教会了你什么？



原文:

Last week I had the pleasure of talking about the SOLID principles as part of Women Who Code London Object Oriented Programming track.

It was fun. It was also extremely challenging.

First, before that I had only ever given internal technical presentations within my company. Giving an external facing technical talk was definitely nerve-wrecking. This SOLID stuff is fairly complex, and the anxiety was real. Public speaking is scary for most of us, though I’m definitely getting better at managing the anxiety around it.

Second, as a self-taught engineer, I constantly grapple with self-doubt and impostor syndrome. What if there is an error in my code sample? What if I butcher an explanation and someone misunderstands a principle? What if I receive a question I don’t know the answer to? These worries are always there, though I try to ignore them and just do my absolute best to prepare. If I do everything I can to deliver the best content possible, then I can be safe in knowing that I have done everything inside my control and just let go of everything else. At least that’s my hope.

And third, life happened. When I was giving this webinar, I happened to be cat-sitting with a cute young kitten who apparently got overexcited when I started talking about SOLID (who can blame him, right?), and decided that jumping on my keyboard and biting my legs was the way to share in the fun. I put on the best poker face I could, but still I was frustrated and a bit angry. I had spent weeks preparing a perfect speech, and now I had to deal with something unexpected and annoying that was throwing me off.

Nevertheless, the talk went well, the participants enjoyed it, my explanations were clear and well-paced, and I had a good time as well.

All of this got me thinking. So much is happening in the lives of the people who put themselves out there, online, to teach or entertain us, stuff that is not always visible, but still presents a challenge for them. We never see any of this, yet we still judge them and spend way more time criticising their failures than praising their successes.

Going through experiences like this teaches me about empathy towards others and what they are going through, but also about resilience and being kinder to myself.

What has public speaking taught you?

Items.Title: 在BBMAs的红地毯上，哪些名人展示了PDA？ | On the red carpet for the BBMAs, which celebrities displayed PDA?
Items.Description:
在 2022 年的告示牌音乐奖上，似乎爱在空气中。 至少，这是与会者得到的印象。

在红地毯上，许多名人选择炫耀他们的约会，而不是穿着他们的设计师服装摆姿势拍照。 机关枪凯利单膝跪地为他的“妻子”梅根福克斯拍照（他说的，不是我们！）； 你可以称之为爱，或者你可以称之为虚假的拍照机会，但无论哪种方式，你都不能否认它确实如此。 在拉斯维加斯的米高梅大花园竞技场内，这种嗡嗡声仍在继续，Olivia Rodrigo、Drake、Taylor Swift、BTS、Doja Cat、Mary J. Blige 和 Dua Lipa（仅举几例）等音乐家都在这里回家奖项。

很明显，有快乐的理由。 查看下面的画廊，查看 2022 年公告牌音乐奖中所有最可爱的情侣照片（以及额外的红地毯造型）。



原文:

At the 2022 Billboard Music Awards, it seemed as though love was in the air. At the very least, this was the impression that attendees got.

On the red carpet, many famous people chose to show off their dates rather than pose in their designer outfits for photos. Machine Gun Kelly got down on one knee to take photos of his "wife" Megan Fox (he said it, not us!); Travis Scott tenderly wrapped his arms around Kylie Jenner; and Michael Buble gently cradled his wife Luisana Lopilato's baby bump. PDA adds a bit of excitement to an activity that trap the cat, other than that, is pretty routine. You can call it love, or you can call it a phony photo opportunity, but either way, you can't deny that it does. And that buzz continued inside of the MGM Grand Garden Arena in Las Vegas, which is where musicians such as Olivia Rodrigo, Drake, Taylor Swift, BTS, Doja Cat, Mary J. Blige, and Dua Lipa (to name a few) took home awards.

It was abundantly clear that there was cause for joy. Check out the gallery below to see all of the most adorable couple photos (along with additional red carpet looks) from the 2022 Billboard Music Awards.
Items.Author: resolvefossil

Items.Title: 如何拥有健康和可持续的在线社区 | How to have healthy and sustainable online communities
Items.Description:
在当今时代，许多人躲在键盘后面。 我们的一些在线社区已成为嘲笑他人的场所，而不是欢迎、鼓励和教导他人的空间。

它指出，那些健康和可持续的社区具有几个关键特征。

我在最近的一篇博文中谈到了优秀开源社区的一些特征。

现在我想谈谈这与在线社区论坛有何关系。 我将要讨论的一些内容与GitHub Discussions直接相关。 然而，其中许多概念与平台无关。 您可以将这些东西应用到您正在运行的任何在线社区； 最重要的事情

在线社区非常适合将人们聚集在一起。 它们是多样化的，不受地理和时区的限制。 但无论您管理或参与哪个社区，您都应该确保您的社区是一个每个人都可以进行良好、相互尊重的对话的空间。

每个人都应该像对待物理空间一样对待在线社区。 如果您在社区中进行对话和讨论，则适用相同的规则，就好像您与某人面对面交谈一样。 尊重他人，鼓励开放式对话，包容并保持好奇心。

以下做法适用于在线社区，但每个社区都有对应的物理世界。 只记得最重要的事情； 对待别人就像你希望被对待一样。

无论您加入哪个社区，请确保您了解社区的目的是什么。 阅读社区指南，并阅读公告。 了解该社区存在的原因以及您如何做出贡献。



原文:

In today's day and age, many people hide behind a keyboard. Some of our online communities have become places to mock others, instead of a space to welcome, encourage, and teach others.

GitHub's State of the Octoverse report talked about the importance of online communities. It pointed out that those communities who are healthy and sustainable are characterised by a few key features.

I talked about some of the characteristics of good open source communities in one of my recent blog posts.

Now I want to talk about how this relates to online community forums. Some of the things I'll talk about are directly related to GitHub Discussions. Many of these concepts however are platform agnostic. You can apply these things to whatever online community you are running; Discord, Slack, Reddit, Twitter, Facebook, whatever you are using.

The most important thing

Online communities are great for bringing people together. They are diverse, unconstrained by geography and timezones. But no matter what community you are managing or participating in, you should ensure your community is a space where everyone can have good, respectful conversations.

Everyone should treat online communities in the same manner as you would in the physical space. If you are having conversations and discussions in your communities, the same rules apply as if you were talking to someone face to face. Treat people with respect, encourage open ended conversations, be inclusive, and be curious.

The practices below apply to online communities, but each has a physical world counterpart. Just remember the most important thing; treat others as you would want to be treated.

1. Read before starting

No matter what community you are joining, make sure you understand what the community's purpose is. Read the community guidelines, and read the announcements. Understand why that community exists and how you can contribute.

Before you start posting things, take the time to not only read through all the "welcome" docs, but also a moment to search and read the forum topics and threads. Don't just start posting without seeing if your conversation has already been started.

Only after you've done both of these things should you make your first post.

2. Mark answers

In a forum style setting, make sure you mark correct answers. If you come across a comment that is correct or helpful, mark it as the answer so others can quickly find solutions too. Communities are all about helping one another, so make sure you're helping put the correct information front and center.

3. Upvote helpful comments

Communities are driven by just that - the community. Upvote answers, comments, and threads to ensure others in the community can find the correct answers, helpful information, or interesting facts.

This will enable your community to grow organically and stay on topic.

4. Threads

Nobody wants to come into a community and try to piece together a conversation. That's why you and your community should organise your conversations into threads. Many platforms such as Facebook, GitHub Discussions, and Reddit have a "reply" feature where users can reply directly to a comment and continue the conversation.

This will ensure your discussions are organised, and new members can easily follow each conversation.

5. Topics or Categories

Members want to come to a community and understand what's going on. Online platforms have features to help you organise your conversations. GitHub Discussions has categories, Discord and Slack have channels, Reddit has flairs, Facebook has tags. Make sure you are posting conversations in their correct topic. This will make it easier for people to find answers, questions, and discussions that suit.

6. Keep topics on track

Finally, once you've chosen the correct category or channel to post in, keep that discussion on track. If you deviate from the original intended conversation, start a new conversation in the correct category. This will ensure the posts are useful and discoverable.

Watch the Video on GitHub Discussions

Many of the things described above relate to all types on online community platforms. Watch the video below to see how they relate to GitHub Discussions:
Items.Author: mishmanners

Items.Title: 使用这些 Ruby on Rails Gem 启动您的 MVP 开发 | Kickstart Your MVP Development With These Ruby on Rails Gems
Items.Description:
如今，构建和拥有一个数字产品很漂亮，当你想构建一个数字产品时，有什么比 MVP 更好的选择 - 最小可行产品！

让 MVP 在竞争中领先于所有人的最大优势之一是快速接触到受众，而不会过度消耗您的资源。

但是，要在市场上发布成熟的产品，您需要一个广泛的后端来首先构建一个强大的结构。

当谈到面向项目的强大后端技术时，Ruby on Rails 开发是您不可忽视的！

为 MVP 开发选择正确的技术或 Ruby on Rails 开发公司变得至关重要。

对于每家初创公司来说，起步阶段对于构建任何产品或进入市场的成本效益来说都是至关重要的。

现在有人会想，为什么只选择 Ruby on Rails 作为 MVP？

多种原因可以支持我的论点，即 Ruby on Rails 是 MVP 后端技术的理想选择。

  • 首先，原型制作过程可能很快。
  • 现代开源语言可以为您的初始启动阶段带来福音，因为它可以以最特殊的方式降低您的成本。
  • 处理您的 MVP 安全方面
  • 出色的灵活性
  • 自动化测试设施

此外，最好的 Ruby on Rails Gems 之一的存在可以最大限度地改进您的 MVP 开发过程。

让我们找出哪些宝石可以证明对您的 MVP 有用！

用于 MVP 开发的最佳 Ruby on Rails Gems 和工具

对于认证过程

  1. 誓言
  2. 机架箱
  3. 设计
  4. 全能认证

用于自动化测试

  1. 规范
  2. 工厂机器人

对于编码风格

  1. 战警
  2. 左勾拳

对于调试过程

蜜蜂

为确保数据安全

  1. 刹车手
  2. 捆绑器审计
  3. 机架攻击
  4. 安全标头

因此，我们已经涵盖了 Web 开发的一些领域，但是您可以在此处获得有关Ruby on Rails 服务如何为您的 MVP 产品提供帮助的详细信息。

毫无疑问，Ruby on Rails Gems 可以出色地处理您的项目，但获得专业人士的帮助可以让您优化这项技术，远远超出您的想象。

Items.Title: Kotlinでfatjar(shadowjar)を作る(kts版) | Kotlinでfatjar(shadowjar)を作る(kts版)
Items.Description:
毎回fatjarを作るときにやり方ググるのが面倒なので自分の记事としてメモしておきます。
なおGradleはkts版とします。Groovy版では少々记述を変える需要があります

参考

なお、ここまでfatjarと书いていますが、実际に作成に使用するのはshadowjarです。

手顺

新しいバージョンが常に出ているはずなので、最新のバージョンはこちらで确认しましょう
构建.gradle.kts

 plugins { kotlin ( "jvm" ) version "1.6.20" // ここはもともと書いてあるはずid ( "com.github.johnrengelman.shadow" ) version "7.1.2" id ( "java" ) } // jarファイルを実行時に実行されるメインファイルのクラスを指定する。 val jar by tasks . getting ( Jar :: class ) { manifest { // mainメソッドが存在するファイルのパスに、末尾にKtを付与したものです。 attributes [ "Main-Class" ] = "packagename.XxxxxXxxxxKt" } }

こうすることで、

でbuild/libs/xxxxxxxxx-1.0-SNAPSHOT-all.jarにshadowJarが生成されます。このjarを以下のように実行すると

java -jar build/libs/xxxxxxxxx-1.0-SNAPSHOT-all.jar

先程指定したpackagename.XxxxxXxxxxKtのmainメソッドが実行されます。

もしほかの主メソッドを実行したい场合は

java -cp build/libs/xxxxxxxxx-1.0-SNAPSHOT-all.jar packagename.YyyyyyYyyyyyKt

のように実行することができます。この场合、 build.gradle.ktsのjar manifestの记载は不要になります。(そわりjava -jarでは実行できす)

以上です。shadowJarを使うだけであれば、

瓦迪姆·阿塔马年科 - May 17 5月17日



原文:

毎回fatjarを作るときにやり方ググるのが面倒なので自分の記事としてメモしておきます。
なおGradleはkts版とします。Groovy版では少々記述を変える必要があります

参考

fatjarとは依存ライブラリも含めたjarファイルです。通常のbuildをした場合に生成されるjarファイルは依存ライブラリは含まれていなく、そのjarを実行する際には依存ライブラリを指定してあげる必要があります。 fatjarを生成しておくと、このjarファイルだけでプログラムの実行が可能となります。

なお、ここまでfatjarと書いていますが、実際に作成に使用するのはshadowjarです。

手順

新しいバージョンが常に出ているはずなので、最新のバージョンはこちらで確認しましょう
https://imperceptiblethoughts.com/shadow/getting-started/

build.gradle.kts

plugins { kotlin("jvm") version "1.6.20" // ここはもともと書いてあるはず id("com.github.johnrengelman.shadow") version "7.1.2" id("java")
} // jarファイルを実行時に実行されるメインファイルのクラスを指定する。
val jar by tasks.getting(Jar::class) { manifest { // mainメソッドが存在するファイルのパスに、末尾にKtを付与したものです。 attributes["Main-Class"] = "packagename.XxxxxXxxxxKt" }
}

こうすることで、

でbuild/libs/xxxxxxxxx-1.0-SNAPSHOT-all.jarにshadowJarが生成されます。 このjarを以下のように実行すると

java -jar build/libs/xxxxxxxxx-1.0-SNAPSHOT-all.jar

先程指定したpackagename.XxxxxXxxxxKtのmainメソッドが実行されます。

もしほかのmainメソッドを実行したい場合は

java -cp build/libs/xxxxxxxxx-1.0-SNAPSHOT-all.jar packagename.YyyyyyYyyyyyKt

のように実行することができます。この場合、build.gradle.ktsのjar manifestの記載は不要になります。(その代わりjava -jarでは実行できない)

以上です。shadowJarを使うだけであれば、

Vadim Atamanenko - May 17

Items.Title: 为什么使用 Ruby on Rails？ | Why Use Ruby on Rails?
Items.Description:
我们生活在技术驱动的世界中，对于企业来说，了解哪种技术最适合下一代 Web 应用程序非常重要。 今天，我们将讨论为什么我们应该使用 ruby​​ on rails 以及 ruby​​ on rails 的哪些特性将有助于业务进一步发展。 此外，我们还将进一步讨论项目类型和 Ruby on rails 的流行组合。

为什么选择 Ruby on Rails？

  • 简单
  • 可扩展性
  • 快速开发/更少编码
  • 发电机/模块/引擎/宝石
  • 简单的自动化测试工具（集成的和外部的）
  • 敏捷项目开发的 RoR
  • 社区支持

可以使用 Ruby on Rails 开发的项目类型

  • 用于 MVP 的 Ruby on Rails
  • 医疗保健应用
  • 跨平台应用
  • 电子商务应用
  • 金融科技应用

前端框架与 Ruby on Rails 的最佳组合。

  1. 此外，我们进一步讨论了一些关于 Ruby on Rails 的常见误区，这肯定会消除您对为什么在下一个项目中使用 ruby​​ on rails的所有疑问。



原文:

We are living in the technology driven world and for business, it is very important to know which technology is best suitable for next-gen web applications. Ruby on rails is among the top in-demand frameworks which is used for backend development.

Today, we are going to discuss why we should use ruby on rails and what features of ruby on rails will help business to grow further. Also, further we will discuss the types of projects and popular combinations of Ruby on rails.

Why Ruby on Rails?

  • Simplicity
  • Ruby on Rails Security
  • Scalability
  • Swift Development / Less Coding
  • Generators / Modules / Engine / Gems
  • Easy Automated Testing Tools (Integrated and External Both)
  • RoR For Agile Project Development
  • Community Support

Type of Project You Can Develop With Ruby on Rails

  • Ruby on Rails For MVP
  • Health Care Application
  • Cross-Platform Applications
  • Ecommerce Application
  • Fintech Application

Best Combination of Front-end Framework with Ruby on Rails.

  1. Angular + Ruby on Rails
  2. ReactJS + Ruby on Rails(RoR)
  3. Vue.JS + Rails

Also, further we discussed some common myths about Ruby on Rails which will surely clear all your doubts of why use ruby on rails for your next project.

Items.Title: 企业应用程序开发的首选——Node.js | Most preferred choice for enterprise applications development - Node.js
Items.Description:
我们可以观察到，出于各种原因，企业正在采用 NodeJs 作为他们的后端技术来提升他们的 Web 应用程序。大多数人担心该技术的未来范围以及它的可扩展性、可靠性和安全性。这里深入了解它为用户服务的可扩展性、可靠性和安全级别似乎非常重要。
让我们通过以下描述讨论 node.js 的一些实现：

在使用 Node.js 进行前端开发时，将来扩展业务会容易得多，而无需更改前端。 容器化和 Node Js 的微服务设计提供了灵活性和可扩展性。
使用 Node 的 Uber 是 Node 可扩展性的实时演示。 即使在高峰时段和假日季节，Node.js 在出租车公司不断发展壮大的网络中的表现也令人钦佩。

由于 Node 包管理器，Node 必须非常可靠。 它还带有一个加密库，以确保您的数据安全。 此外，您可以依靠强大的 Node.js 社区来帮助您克服和解决问题。

可以重用 Web 应用程序代码来创建适用于 macOS、Windows 和 Linux 的桌面应用程序。 此外，Web 应用程序开发人员可以在桌面应用程序上工作，而无需学习其他语言，如 Objective-C、C# 或其他类似语言。

因此，我们可以看到这些是证明为什么使用 node.js并证明为什么它是商业应用程序开发的最佳选择的一系列好处。



原文:

We can observe that for a variety of reasons, businesses are adopting NodeJs as their backend technology to boost their web applications.Most of the people are worried about the future scope of the technology and it’'s scalability, dependability, and security.Here it seems very much important to know in depth about the scalability, dependability and level of security it serves to its users.
Let’s discuss some of those fulfillments of node.js with below description:

Is Node.js a Scalable Framework?

While using Node.js for your frontend development, it will be much easier to expand your business in the future without having to change the frontend. Containerization and Node Js' microservices design provide flexibility and scalability.
Uber, which uses Node, is a real-time demo of Node's scalability. Even during peak hours and holiday seasons, Node.js is performing admirably in the cab company's ever-growing and increasing network.

Is Node.js a Reliable Framework?

Because of the Node Package Manager, Node must be extremely reliable. NPM is a default Node.js tool that comes with every installation. NPM modules for Node js are comparable to Ruby Gems in that they are open source, easy to install, and reusable components with version and dependency management.

The Node package manager makes Node extremely dependable by providing a wide range of options.

Is Node.js a secure framework?

Node Js includes support for the HTTPS protocol, which allows your application to open securely and avoid being exposed. It also comes with a crypto library to keep your data safe. Furthermore, you may rely on the tremendously strong Node.js community to help you overcome and resolve your issues.

One of the main reasons Node.js is so popular in enterprise app development is that it facilitates the creation of cross-platform apps for desktop users. Web app code can be reused to create desktop apps for macOS, Windows, and Linux. Additionally, web app developers can work on desktop apps without having to learn extra languages like Objective-C, C#, or other similar languages.

Thus we can see that these are the list of benefits proving why use node.js and justify why it the best choice for a commercial app development.
