Items.Title: 在 Go 中同步包 | Sync package in Go
Items.Description:
正如我们之前所了解的，goroutines 运行在相同的地址空间中，因此对共享内存的访问必须是同步的。 等待组

主 goroutine 调用Add来设置要等待的 goroutine 的数量。 然后每个 goroutine 运行并在完成时调用Done 。 同时， Wait可以用来阻塞，直到所有的 goroutine 都完成。

用法

我们可以通过以下方法使用sync.WaitGroup ：

  • 这必须在我们执行 goroutine 之前调用。
  • 在 goroutine 中调用Done()以表明 goroutine 已成功执行。
  • 例子

    让我们看一个例子

    package main import ( "fmt" "sync" ) func work () { fmt . Println ( "working..." ) } func main () { var wg sync . WaitGroup wg . Add ( 1 ) go func () { defer wg . Done () work () }() wg . Wait () }

    如果我们运行它，我们可以看到我们的程序按预期运行

    $ go run main.go working...

    我们也可以直接将 waitgroup 传递给函数。

     func work ( wg * sync . WaitGroup ) { defer wg . Done () fmt . Println ( "working..." ) } func main () { var wg sync . WaitGroup wg . Add ( 1 ) go work ( & wg ) wg . Wait () }

    但重要的是要知道第一次使用后不得复制等待组。 如果它被显式传递给函数，它应该由一个指针来完成。 这是因为它会影响我们的计数器，从而破坏我们程序的逻辑。

    让我们也增加 goroutine 的数量并更新我们的 waitgroup Add方法以等待 4 个 goroutine。

     func main () { var wg sync . WaitGroup wg . Add ( 4 ) go work ( & wg ) go work ( & wg ) go work ( & wg ) go work ( & wg ) wg . Wait () }

    正如预期的那样，我们所有的 goroutine 都被执行了。

     $ go run main.go working... working... working... working...

    互斥体

    互斥锁是一种互斥锁，可防止其他进程进入数据的关键部分，而进程占用它以防止发生竞争条件。

    什么是临界区？

    因此，临界区可以是一段不能由多个线程同时运行的代码，因为该代码包含共享资源。

    用法

    我们可以通过以下方法使用sync.Mutex ：

    • 例子

      让我们看一个例子，我们将创建一个Counter结构并添加一个Update方法来更新内部值。

       package main import ( "fmt" "sync" ) type Counter struct { value int } func ( c * Counter ) Update ( n int , wg * sync . WaitGroup ) { defer wg . Done () fmt . Printf ( "Adding %d to %d 
 " , n , c . value ) c . value += n } func main () { var wg sync . WaitGroup c := Counter {} wg . Add ( 4 ) go c . Update ( 10 , & wg ) go c . Update ( - 5 , & wg ) go c . Update ( 25 , & wg ) go c . Update ( 19 , & wg ) wg . Wait () fmt . Println ( c . value ) }

      让我们运行它，看看会发生什么。

       $ go run main.go Adding -5 to 0 Adding 10 to 0 Adding 19 to 0 Adding 25 to 0 Result is 49

      这看起来不准确，似乎我们的值始终为零，但我们以某种方式得到了正确的答案。

      嗯，这是因为，在我们的示例中，多个 goroutine 正在更新value变量。 正如您一定猜到的那样，这并不理想。

      这是 Mutex 的完美用例。 因此，让我们从使用sync.Mutex开始，并将我们的关键部分包装在Lock()和Unlock()方法之间。

       package main import ( "fmt" "sync" ) type Counter struct { m sync . Mutex value int } func ( c * Counter ) Update ( n int , wg * sync . WaitGroup ) { c . m . Lock () defer wg . Done () fmt . Printf ( "Adding %d to %d 
 " , n , c . value ) c . value += n c . m . Unlock () } func main () { var wg sync . WaitGroup c := Counter {} wg . Add ( 4 ) go c . Update ( 10 , & wg ) go c . Update ( - 5 , & wg ) go c . Update ( 25 , & wg ) go c . Update ( 19 , & wg ) wg . Wait () }
       $ go run main.go Adding -5 to 0 Adding 19 to -5 Adding 25 to 14 Adding 10 to 39 Result is 49

      看起来我们解决了我们的问题，输出看起来也正确。

      注意：与 WaitGroup 类似，Mutex 在首次使用后不得复制。

      锁可以由任意数量的读取器或单个写入器持有。

      换句话说，读者不必互相等待。 他们只需要等待持有锁的写入者。

      因此， sync.RWMutex更适用于大部分读取的数据，与sync.Mutex相比，节省的资源是时间。

      用法

      与sync.Mutex类似，我们可以通过以下方法使用sync.RWMutex ：

      • 请注意 RWMutex 与 Mutex 相比如何具有额外RLock和RUnlock方法。

        例子

        让我们添加一个读取计数器值的GetValue方法。 我们还将sync.Mutex更改为sync.RWMutex

        现在，我们可以简单地使用RLock和RUnlock方法，这样读者就不必互相等待了。

         package main import ( "fmt" "sync" "time" ) type Counter struct { m sync . RWMutex value int } func ( c * Counter ) Update ( n int , wg * sync . WaitGroup ) { defer wg . Done () c . m . Lock () fmt . Printf ( "Adding %d to %d 
 " , n , c . value ) c . value += n c . m . Unlock () } func ( c * Counter ) GetValue ( wg * sync . WaitGroup ) { defer wg . Done () c . m . RLock () defer c . m . RUnlock () fmt . Println ( "Get value:" , c . value ) time . Sleep ( 400 * time . Millisecond ) } func main () { var wg sync . WaitGroup c := Counter {} wg . Add ( 4 ) go c . Update ( 10 , & wg ) go c . GetValue ( & wg ) go c . GetValue ( & wg ) go c . GetValue ( & wg ) wg . Wait () }
         $ go run main.go Get value: 0 Adding 10 to 0 Get value: 10 Get value: 10

        注意： sync.Mutex和sync.RWMutex都实现了sync.Locker接口：

         type Locker interface { Lock () Unlock () }

        条件

        当共享资源的状态发生变化时，它可以用来通知被互斥锁阻塞的 goroutine。

        每个 Cond 都有一个关联的锁（通常是*Mutex或*RWMutex ），在更改条件和调用 Wait 方法时必须持有该锁。

        但我们为什么需要它？

        一种情况可能是一个进程正在接收数据，而其他进程必须等待该进程接收数据才能读取正确的数据。

        如果我们简单地使用通道或互斥体，则只有一个进程可以等待并读取数据。 没有办法通知其他进程读取数据。 因此，我们可以通过sync.Cond来协调共享资源。

        用法

        例子

        这是一个使用Cond演示不同 goroutine 交互的示例

        package main import ( "fmt" "sync" "time" ) var done = false func read ( name string , c * sync . Cond ) { c . L . Lock () for ! done { c . Wait () } fmt . Println ( name , "starts reading" ) c . L . Unlock () } func write ( name string , c * sync . Cond ) { fmt . Println ( name , "starts writing" ) time . Sleep ( time . Second ) c . L . Lock () done = true c . L . Unlock () fmt . Println ( name , "wakes all" ) c . Broadcast () } func main () { var m sync . Mutex cond := sync . NewCond ( & m ) go read ( "Reader 1" , cond ) go read ( "Reader 2" , cond ) go read ( "Reader 3" , cond ) write ( "Writer" , cond ) time . Sleep ( 4 * time . Second ) }
         $ go run main.go Writer starts writing Writer wakes all Reader 2 starts reading Reader 3 starts reading Reader 1 starts reading

        正如我们所见，读者使用Wait方法暂停，直到作者使用Broadcast方法唤醒进程。

        一次

        一次确保即使在多个 goroutine 中也只会执行一次。

        用法

        与其他原语不同， sync.Once只有一个方法：

        • 如果Do被多次调用，只有第一次调用会调用函数f

        例子

        这看起来很简单，让我们举个例子

        package main import ( "fmt" "sync" ) func main () { var count int increment := func () { count ++ } var once sync . Once var increments sync . WaitGroup increments . Add ( 100 ) for i := 0 ; i < 100 ; i ++ { go func () { defer increments . Done () once . Do ( increment ) }() } increments . Wait () fmt . Printf ( "Count is %d 
 " , count ) }
         $ go run main.go Count is 1

        正如我们所见，即使我们运行了 100 个 goroutine，计数也只增加了一次。

        水池

        可以随时删除池中的任何存储值，而无需收到通知。 另外，在高负载下，对象池可以动态扩展，当不使用或者并发不高时，对象池会收缩。

        关键思想是对象的重用，避免重复创建和销毁，这会影响性能。

        但我们为什么需要它？

        也就是说，它使构建高效、线程安全的空闲列表变得容易。 但是，它并不适用于所有空闲列表。

        池提供了一种将分配开销成本分散到许多客户端的方法。

        需要注意的是，Pool 也有其性能成本。 使用sync.Pool比简单的初始化要慢得多。 此外，第一次使用后不得复制池。

        用法

        例子

        现在，让我们看一个例子。

        首先，我们将创建一个新的sync.Pool ，我们可以选择指定一个函数来在调用Get时生成一个值，否则它将返回一个nil值。

         package main import ( "fmt" "sync" ) type Person struct { Name string } var pool = sync . Pool { New : func () any { fmt . Println ( "Creating a new person..." ) return & Person {} }, } func main () { person := pool . Get () . ( * Person ) fmt . Println ( "Get object from sync.Pool for the first time:" , person ) fmt . Println ( "Put the object back in the pool" ) pool . Put ( person ) person . Name = "Gopher" fmt . Println ( "Set object property name:" , person . Name ) fmt . Println ( "Get object from pool again (it's updated):" , pool . Get () . ( * Person )) fmt . Println ( "There is no object in the pool now (new one will be created):" , pool . Get () . ( * Person )) }

        如果我们运行它，我们会看到一个有趣的输出：

         $ go run main.go Creating a new person... Get object from sync.Pool for the first time : & {} Put the object back in the pool Set object property name: Gopher Get object from pool again ( it 's updated): &{Gopher} Creating a new person... There is no object in the pool now (new one will be created): &{}

        注意我们在调用Get时是如何进行类型断言的

        可以看出， sync.Pool严格来说是一个临时对象池，适合存放一些将在 goroutine 之间共享的临时对象。

        地图

        加载、存储和删除分布在恒定时间内。

        但我们为什么需要它？

        大多数代码应该使用普通的 Go 映射，使用单独的锁定或协调，以获得更好的类型安全性并更容易维护其他不变量以及映射内容。

        当给定键的条目只被写入一次但被读取多次时，就像在只增长的缓存中一样。

      • 当多个 goroutine 读取、写入和覆盖不相交键集的条目时。 在这两种情况下，与使用单独的Mutex或RWMutex配对的 Go map 相比，使用sync.Map可以显着减少锁争用。

      零地图是空的，可以使用了。 首次使用后不得复制地图。

      用法

      加载的结果报告密钥是否存在。

    • 否则，它存储并返回给定的值。 如果值已加载，则加载结果为 true，如果已存储，则为 false。
    • 如果f返回 false，则范围停止迭代。

    注意：范围不一定对应于地图内容的任何一致快照。

    例子

    让我们看一个例子。 在这里，我们将启动一堆 goroutine，它们将同时从我们的地图中添加和检索值。

     package main import ( "fmt" "sync" ) func main () { var wg sync . WaitGroup var m sync . Map wg . Add ( 10 ) for i := 0 ; i <= 4 ; i ++ { go func ( k int ) { v := fmt . Sprintf ( "value %v" , k ) fmt . Println ( "Writing:" , v ) m . Store ( k , v ) wg . Done () }( i ) } for i := 0 ; i <= 4 ; i ++ { go func ( k int ) { v , _ := m . Load ( k ) fmt . Println ( "Reading: " , v ) wg . Done () }( i ) } wg . Wait () }

    正如预期的那样，我们的存储和检索操作对于并发使用是安全的。

     $ go run main.go Reading: <nil> Writing: value 0 Writing: value 1 Writing: value 2 Writing: value 3 Writing: value 4 Reading: value 0 Reading: value 1 Reading: value 2 Reading: value 3

    原子

    包 atomic 提供用于实现同步算法的整数和指针的低级原子内存原语。

    用法

    添加

  • 加载
  • 店铺
  • 交换
  • 比较和交换

例子

我们将无法在这里涵盖所有功能。 那么，让我们看一下像AddInt32这样最常用的函数来了解一下。

 package main import ( "fmt" "sync" "sync/atomic" ) func add ( w * sync . WaitGroup , num * int32 ) { defer w . Done () atomic . AddInt32 ( num , 1 ) } func main () { var n int32 = 0 var wg sync . WaitGroup wg . Add ( 1000 ) for i := 0 ; i < 1000 ; i = i + 1 { go add ( & wg , & n ) } wg . Wait () fmt . Println ( "Result:" , n ) }

这里atomic.AddInt32保证n的结果为 1000，因为原子操作的指令执行不能被中断。

 go run main.go Result: 1000

好吧，这涵盖了我们关于 Go 中sync包的讨论。 我们下个教程见！



原文:

As we learned earlier, goroutines run in the same address space, so access to shared memory must be synchronized. The sync package provides useful primitives.

WaitGroup

A WaitGroup waits for a collection of goroutines to finish. The main goroutine calls Add to set the number of goroutines to wait for. Then each of the goroutines runs and calls Done when finished. At the same time, Wait can be used to block until all goroutines have finished.

Usage

We can use the sync.WaitGroup using the following methods:

  • Add(delta int) takes in an integer value which is essentially the number of goroutines that the waitgroup has to wait for. This must be called before we execute a goroutine.
  • Done() is called within the goroutine to signal that the goroutine has successfully executed.
  • Wait() blocks the program until all the goroutines specified by Add() have invoked Done() from within.

Example

Let's take a look at an example

package main import ( "fmt" "sync"
) func work() { fmt.Println("working...")
} func main() { var wg sync.WaitGroup wg.Add(1) go func() { defer wg.Done() work() }() wg.Wait()
}

If we run this, we can see our program runs as expected

$ go run main.go
working...

We can also pass the waitgroup to the function directly.

func work(wg *sync.WaitGroup) { defer wg.Done() fmt.Println("working...")
} func main() { var wg sync.WaitGroup wg.Add(1) go work(&wg) wg.Wait()
}

But is important to know that a waitgroup must not be copied after first use. And if it's explicitly passed into functions, it should be done by a pointer. This is because it can affect our counter which will disrupt the logic of our program.

Let's also increase the number of goroutines and update our waitgroup Add method to wait for 4 goroutines.

func main() { var wg sync.WaitGroup wg.Add(4) go work(&wg) go work(&wg) go work(&wg) go work(&wg) wg.Wait()
}

And as expected, all our goroutines were executed.

$ go run main.go
working...
working...
working...
working...

Mutex

A Mutex is a mutual exclusion lock that prevents other processes from entering a critical section of data while a process occupies it to prevent race conditions from happening.

What's a critical section?

So, a critical section can be a piece of code that must not be run by multiple threads at once because the code contains shared resources.

Usage

We can use sync.Mutex using the following methods:

  • Lock() acquires or holds the lock.
  • Unlock() releases the lock.
  • TryLock() tries to lock and reports whether it succeeded.

Example

Let's take a look at an example, we will create a Counter struct and add an Update method which will update the internal value.

package main import ( "fmt" "sync"
) type Counter struct { value int
} func (c *Counter) Update(n int, wg *sync.WaitGroup) { defer wg.Done() fmt.Printf("Adding %d to %d
", n, c.value) c.value += n
} func main() { var wg sync.WaitGroup c := Counter{} wg.Add(4) go c.Update(10, &wg) go c.Update(-5, &wg) go c.Update(25, &wg) go c.Update(19, &wg) wg.Wait() fmt.Println(c.value)
}

Let's run this and see what happens.

$ go run main.go
Adding -5 to 0
Adding 10 to 0
Adding 19 to 0
Adding 25 to 0
Result is 49

That doesn't look accurate, seems like our value is always zero but we somehow got the correct answer.

Well, this is because, in our example, multiple goroutines are updating the value variable. And as you must have guessed, this is not ideal.

This is the perfect use case for Mutex. So, let's start by using sync.Mutex and wrap our critical section in between Lock() and Unlock() methods.

package main import ( "fmt" "sync"
) type Counter struct { m sync.Mutex value int
} func (c *Counter) Update(n int, wg *sync.WaitGroup) { c.m.Lock() defer wg.Done() fmt.Printf("Adding %d to %d
", n, c.value) c.value += n c.m.Unlock()
} func main() { var wg sync.WaitGroup c := Counter{} wg.Add(4) go c.Update(10, &wg) go c.Update(-5, &wg) go c.Update(25, &wg) go c.Update(19, &wg) wg.Wait()
}

$ go run main.go
Adding -5 to 0
Adding 19 to -5
Adding 25 to 14
Adding 10 to 39
Result is 49

Looks like we solved our issue and the output looks correct as well.

Note: Similar to WaitGroup a Mutex must not be copied after first use.

RWMutex

An RWMutex is a reader/writer mutual exclusion lock. The lock can be held by an arbitrary number of readers or a single writer.

In other words, readers don't have to wait for each other. They only have to wait for writers holding the lock.

sync.RWMutex is thus preferable for data that is mostly read, and the resource that is saved compared to a sync.Mutex is time.

Usage

Similar to sync.Mutex, we can use sync.RWMutex using the following methods:

  • Lock() acquires or holds the lock.
  • Unlock() releases the lock.
  • RLock() acquires or holds the read lock.
  • RUnlock() releases the read lock.

Notice how RWMutex has additional RLock and RUnlock methods compared to Mutex.

Example

Let's add a GetValue method which will read the counter value. We will also change sync.Mutex to sync.RWMutex

Now, we can simply use the RLock and RUnlock methods so that readers don't have to wait for each other.

package main import ( "fmt" "sync" "time"
) type Counter struct { m sync.RWMutex value int
} func (c *Counter) Update(n int, wg *sync.WaitGroup) { defer wg.Done() c.m.Lock() fmt.Printf("Adding %d to %d
", n, c.value) c.value += n c.m.Unlock()
} func (c *Counter) GetValue(wg *sync.WaitGroup) { defer wg.Done() c.m.RLock() defer c.m.RUnlock() fmt.Println("Get value:", c.value) time.Sleep(400 * time.Millisecond)
} func main() { var wg sync.WaitGroup c := Counter{} wg.Add(4) go c.Update(10, &wg) go c.GetValue(&wg) go c.GetValue(&wg) go c.GetValue(&wg) wg.Wait()
}

$ go run main.go
Get value: 0
Adding 10 to 0
Get value: 10
Get value: 10

Note: Both sync.Mutex and sync.RWMutex implements the sync.Locker interface:

type Locker interface { Lock() Unlock()
}

Cond

The sync.Cond condition variable can be used to coordinate those goroutines that want to share resources. When the state of shared resources changes, it can be used to notify goroutines blocked by a mutex.

Each Cond has an associated lock (often a *Mutex or *RWMutex), which must be held when changing the condition and when calling the Wait method.

But why do we need it?

One scenario can be when one process is receiving data, and other processes must wait for this process to receive data before they can read the correct data.

If we simply use a channel or mutex, only one process can wait and read the data. There is no way to notify other processes to read the data. Thus, we can sync.Cond to coordinate shared resources.

Usage

sync.Cond comes with the following methods:

  • NewCond(l Locker) returns a new Cond.
  • Broadcast() wakes all goroutines waiting on the condition.
  • Signal() wakes one goroutine waiting on the condition if there is any.
  • Wait() atomically unlocks the underlying mutex lock.

Example

Here is an example that demonstrates the interaction of different goroutines using the Cond

package main import ( "fmt" "sync" "time"
) var done = false func read(name string, c *sync.Cond) { c.L.Lock() for !done { c.Wait() } fmt.Println(name, "starts reading") c.L.Unlock()
} func write(name string, c *sync.Cond) { fmt.Println(name, "starts writing") time.Sleep(time.Second) c.L.Lock() done = true c.L.Unlock() fmt.Println(name, "wakes all") c.Broadcast()
} func main() { var m sync.Mutex cond := sync.NewCond(&m) go read("Reader 1", cond) go read("Reader 2", cond) go read("Reader 3", cond) write("Writer", cond) time.Sleep(4 * time.Second)
}

$ go run main.go
Writer starts writing
Writer wakes all
Reader 2 starts reading
Reader 3 starts reading
Reader 1 starts reading

As we can see, the readers were suspended using the Wait method until the writer used the Broadcast method to wake up the process.

Once

Once ensures that only one execution will be carried out even among several goroutines.

Usage

Unlike other primitives, sync.Once only has a single method:

  • Do(f func()) calls the function f only once. If Do is called multiple times, only the first call will invoke the function f

Example

This seems pretty straightforward, let's take an example

package main import ( "fmt" "sync"
) func main() { var count int increment := func() { count++ } var once sync.Once var increments sync.WaitGroup increments.Add(100) for i := 0; i < 100; i++ { go func() { defer increments.Done() once.Do(increment) }() } increments.Wait() fmt.Printf("Count is %d
", count)
}

$ go run main.go
Count is 1

As we can see, even when we ran 100 goroutines, the count only got incremented once.

Pool

Pool is s a scalable pool of temporary objects and is also concurrency safe. Any stored value in the pool can be deleted at any time without receiving notification. In addition, under high load, the object pool can be dynamically expanded, and when it is not used or the concurrency is not high, the object pool will shrink.

The key idea is the reuse of objects to avoid repeated creation and destruction, which will affect the performance.

But why do we need it?

Pool's purpose is to cache allocated but unused items for later reuse, relieving pressure on the garbage collector. That is, it makes it easy to build efficient, thread-safe free lists. However, it is not suitable for all free lists.

The appropriate use of a Pool is to manage a group of temporary items silently shared among and potentially reused by concurrent independent clients of a package. Pool provides a way to spread the cost of allocation overhead across many clients.

It is important to note that Pool also has its performance cost. It is much slower to use sync.Pool than simple initialization. Also, a Pool must not be copied after first use.

Usage

sync.Pool gives us the following methods:

  • Get() selects an arbitrary item from the Pool, removes it from the Pool, and returns it to the caller.
  • Put(x any) adds the item to the pool

Example

Now, let's look at an example.

First, we will create a new sync.Pool, where we can optionally specify a function to generate a value when we call, Get, otherwise it will return a nil value.

package main import ( "fmt" "sync"
) type Person struct { Name string
} var pool = sync.Pool{ New: func() any { fmt.Println("Creating a new person...") return &Person{} },
} func main() { person := pool.Get().(*Person) fmt.Println("Get object from sync.Pool for the first time:", person) fmt.Println("Put the object back in the pool") pool.Put(person) person.Name = "Gopher" fmt.Println("Set object property name:", person.Name) fmt.Println("Get object from pool again (it's updated):", pool.Get().(*Person)) fmt.Println("There is no object in the pool now (new one will be created):", pool.Get().(*Person))
}

And if we run this, we'll see an interesting output:

$ go run main.go
Creating a new person...
Get object from sync.Pool for the first time: &{}
Put the object back in the pool
Set object property name: Gopher
Get object from pool again (it's updated): &{Gopher}
Creating a new person...
There is no object in the pool now (new one will be created): &{}

Notice how we did type assertion when we call Get

It can be seen that the sync.Pool is strictly a temporary object pool, which is suitable for storing some temporary objects that will be shared among goroutines.

Map

Map is like the standard map[any]any but is safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines without additional locking or coordination. Loads, stores, and deletes are spread over constant time.

But why do we need it?

The Map type is specialized. Most code should use a plain Go map instead, with separate locking or coordination, for better type safety and to make it easier to maintain other invariants along with the map content.

The Map type is optimized for two common use cases:

  • When the entry for a given key is only ever written once but read many times, as in caches that only grow.
  • When multiple goroutines read, write, and overwrite entries for disjoint sets of keys. In these two cases, the use of a sync.Map may significantly reduce lock contention compared to a Go map paired with a separate Mutex or RWMutex.

The zero Map is empty and ready for use. A Map must not be copied after first use.

Usage

sync.Map gives us the following methods:

  • Delete() deletes the value for a key.
  • Load(key any) returns the value stored in the map for a key, or nil if no value is present.
  • LoadAndDelete(key any) deletes the value for a key, returning the previous value if any. The loaded result reports whether the key was present.
  • LoadOrStore(key, value any) returns the existing value for the key if present. Otherwise, it stores and returns the given value. The loaded result is true if the value was loaded, and false if stored.
  • Store(key, value any) sets the value for a key.
  • Range(f func(key, value any) bool) calls f sequentially for each key and value present in the map. If f returns false, the range stops the iteration.

Note: Range does not necessarily correspond to any consistent snapshot of the Map's contents.

Example

Let's look at an example. Here, we will launch a bunch of goroutines that will add and retrieve values from our map concurrently.

package main import ( "fmt" "sync"
) func main() { var wg sync.WaitGroup var m sync.Map wg.Add(10) for i := 0; i <= 4; i++ { go func(k int) { v := fmt.Sprintf("value %v", k) fmt.Println("Writing:", v) m.Store(k, v) wg.Done() }(i) } for i := 0; i <= 4; i++ { go func(k int) { v, _ := m.Load(k) fmt.Println("Reading: ", v) wg.Done() }(i) } wg.Wait()
}

As expected, our store and retrieve operation will be safe for concurrent use.

$ go run main.go
Reading: <nil>
Writing: value 0
Writing: value 1
Writing: value 2
Writing: value 3
Writing: value 4
Reading: value 0
Reading: value 1
Reading: value 2
Reading: value 3

Atomic

Package atomic provides low-level atomic memory primitives for integers and pointers that are useful for implementing synchronization algorithms.

Usage

atomic package provides several functions which do the following 5 operations for int, uint, and uintptr types:

  • Add
  • Load
  • Store
  • Swap
  • Compare and Swap

Example

We won't be able to cover all of the functions here. So, let's take a look at the most commonly used function like AddInt32 to get an idea.

package main import ( "fmt" "sync" "sync/atomic"
) func add(w *sync.WaitGroup, num *int32) { defer w.Done() atomic.AddInt32(num, 1)
} func main() { var n int32 = 0 var wg sync.WaitGroup wg.Add(1000) for i := 0; i < 1000; i = i + 1 { go add(&wg, &n) } wg.Wait() fmt.Println("Result:", n)
}

Here, atomic.AddInt32 guarantees that the result of n will be 1000 as the instruction execution of atomic operations cannot be interrupted.

go run main.go
Result: 1000

Well, this covers our discussion about the sync package in Go. I'll see you in the next tutorial!
Items.Author: karanpratapsingh

Items.Title: K8s 对象 - 第 2 部分 [部署] | K8s Objects - Part 2 [Deployment]
Items.Description:
在这篇博客中，让我们来看看常用的 K8s 对象之一，部署！

部署

它允许用户在清单文件中声明所需的状态，并且控制器将确保当前状态与部署后声明的状态相同。

因此，让我们在此处了解如何创建和使用 Kubernetes 部署、管理它们、横向扩展和回滚部署。

像往常一样，开始使用 Kubernetes 配置/清单文件。

部署-demo.yaml

让我们首先创建一个包含四个副本的简单 nginx 部署。

如前所述，部署在下面使用副本集，配置文件与副本集非常相似。

有一个附加标签来匹配副本集选择器标签并将其作为部署。

让我们创建部署并验证如下工作，

通过这种配置，我们创建了一个包含 4 个 Pod 的副本集，其中 Nginx 容器暴露在端口 80 上——作为部署进行控制。

从为提供的 Pod 配置创建所需数量的副本到确保对数量进行管理……部署似乎与 Replicaset 相同。 实际上，通过创建此部署也创建了一个副本集。

但部署不仅仅是副本集，它还不止于此。
现在，让我们来尝尝Deployment的真谛吧！

部署的力量

与副本集不同，Deployment 使您能够更新 pod 配置，而不会对底层应用程序造成任何中断。

以我们的示例为例，假设您出于某种原因需要更改图像。 在副本集中更改配置文件中的版本将产生零影响，因为副本集只是确保所需数量的 Pod 正在运行。 它不担心配置部分。 虽然，这在部署中得到了小心处理……我仔细重复一遍！

这将在使用新配置创建 pod 之前删除所有现有的 pod。

  • 它会在一定程度上影响应用程序的可用性。
  • 如果创建 Pod 时出现问题（比如无效的镜像名称），这将导致运行 Pod 的次数为零 - 完全关闭应用程序，直到问题得到解决并应用新的部署。

    • 这将以滚动更新的方式重新创建 Pod。

  • 它将逐渐删除和重新创建 pod，而不会中断应用程序的可用性。
  • 您可以控制在此期间的任何给定时间点您希望可用的 Pod 的百分比。 这是通过称为maxUnavailable和maxSurge maxUnavailable 的属性实现的，maxUnavailable - 是指示在更新过程中可能不可用的最大 pod 数量的百分比。 如果创建 Pod 时出现问题（比如无效的镜像名称），只有 maxUnavailable 数量的 Pod 将不可用（比如 maxUnavailable 为 25%，那么在 4 个 Pod 中只有 1 个不可用），直到问题得到解决。

    • 您可以在配置文件的spec部分定义此策略及其规范。 默认情况下，RollingUpdate 提供了如下所示的规范

    让我们在行动中检查一下。

    更改配置：将 nginx 映像版本更改为 1.20。 从 1:21 开始（写这篇博客的最新时间）

    让我们应用上述配置并检查此部署的 ROLLOUT

    如您所见，由于策略类型是具有默认规范的 RollingUpdate，因此更改（图像版本）的推出逐渐发生（maxSurge 为 25%），旧图像的终止遵循相同的数字（maxUnavailable 为 25%）。

    对于 Deployment 配置的每次“新”更新，都会创建一个新的副本集。 例如，当您第一次拥有 nginx 映像 1:21 的部署文件时 - 创建了一个副本集。 稍后当您将版本更新为 1:20 时，会创建另一个副本集。 但是当你应用旧版本的 Deployment 配置时，副本集会被重用！ 您可以暂停和恢复部署！ 这使我们能够修改和尝试任何配置/修复，而无需创建新的 ReplicaSet 部署。

    您可以通过以下方式暂停部署，

    kubectl rollout pause deploy deployment-demo

    暂停后，假设我们更新了 Pod 的镜像版本。
    如您所见，推出并未开始。 它保持暂停状态。

    为了恢复推出使用，

    kubectl rollout resume deploy deployment-demo

    恢复后，通过检查部署状态，我们可以了解到，一旦应用了恢复命令，被保持/暂停的部署过程就会自动恢复。

    您还可以将部署回滚到以前的版本。 这是一个关键功能，允许我们撤消部署中的更改！！

    例如，假设集群中部署了一个生产错误，我们可以简单地将部署回滚到以前的版本，这只是一个命令，无需停机，直到错误得到修复！

    回滚到以前的版本...是的，这里有人帮助我们！ 这是部署控制器的历史功能。

    每个部署都在历史记录中进行跟踪。



    原文:

    In this blog let's checkout one of the commonly used K8s Objects, Deployment!

    Deployment

    K8s Deployment are used to define and manage the Pods, they work wrapping the replicaset mechanism underneath.
    It allows users to declare the desired state in the manifest file, and the controller will ensure that the current state is same as the declared state once deployed.

    So, let’s check out on how to create and use Kubernetes deployments, manage them, scale in-out, and roll back deployments here.

    As usual, getting started with the Kubernetes configuration / manifest file.

    deployment-demo.yaml

    Let’s first create a simple nginx deployment with four replicas.

    As mentioned, deployment uses replicaset underneath, the configuration file is very similar to the replicaset.

    There is an additional label to match the replicaset selector label and have it as a deployment.

    Let's create the deployment and verify the working as below,

    By this configuration, we have created a replicaset of 4 Pods with Nginx containers exposed at port 80 - that is controlled as a deployment.

    From creating desired number of replicas of provided Pod configuration to ensuring the number is managed... deployment seems to be same as Replicaset. In fact there is a replicaset created as well by creating this deployment.

    But deployment is not just replicaset, it's more than that.
    Now, let's taste the real essence of Deployment!

    Power of Deployment

    => Modify your Pod configuration on the fly!

    Unlike replicaset, Deployment powers you to update the pod configuration without causing any interruption to the underlying application.

    Going by our example, say you need to change the image for some reason. In replicaset changing the version in the configuration file will have nil impact as replicaset just ensures the desired number of Pods are running. It doesn't worry on the configuration part. Whilst, this is handled carefully in Deployments... I repeat carefully!

    There are two types of strategies used by K8s deployment for managing the Pod change rollout.

    -> Recreate ::

    • This will delete all the existing pods before creating the pods with new configuration.
    • It will affect the availability of the application to some extend.
    • If there is some problem with Pod creation (Say invalid image name), this would end up with zero running Pods - Taking down the application completely down until the problem is fixed and new deployment is applied.

    -> RollingUpdate ::

    • This will recreate the pods in a rolling update manner.
    • It will delete and recreate pods gradually without interrupting the application availability.
    • You have control over the percentage of Pods you want to be available at any given point of time during the time. This is achieved through properties called maxUnavailable and maxSurge maxUnavailable - is the percentage indicating maximum number of pods that can be unavailable during the update process. maxSurge - is the percentage indicating maximum number of pods that can be created during the update process.
    • If there is some problem with Pod creation (Say invalid image name), maxUnavailable number of Pods only will be unavailable (Say maxUnavailable is 25%, then out of 4 Pods only 1 will go unavailable) until the problem is fixed.

    You can define this strategy and its specifications in spec section of the configuration file. By default the RollingUpdate is provided with specifications as shown below

    Let's check this out in action.

    Change to configuration: Changing the nginx image version to 1.20. from 1:21 (latest when this blog was written)

    Let's apply the above configuration and check the ROLLOUT of this deployment

    As you can see since the strategy type is RollingUpdate with default specifications, the rollout of the change (Image version) happens gradually one by one (maxSurge is 25%) and termination of old image follows the same number (maxUnavailable is 25%).

    <-- TidBit -->
    For every "new" update of the Deployment configuration, a new replicaset is created. For example, when you first have deployment file with nginx image 1:21 - a replicaset is created. Later when you updated the version to 1:20, another replicaset is created. But when you apply the older version of Deployment configuration, the replicaset is reused! (Yes, it's that intelligent 😛)

    => Pause / Resume

    You can pause and resume deployment! This enables us to modify and try out any configs / fixes without creating a new ReplicaSet rollout.

    You can pause the deployment by,

    kubectl rollout pause deploy deployment-demo

    After pausing, say we update the image version of the Pods.
    As you can see, the rollout doesn’t start. It remains in the paused state.

    In order to resume the rollout use,

    kubectl rollout resume deploy deployment-demo

    Once resumed, by checking the rollout status we can understand that as soon as resume command is applied, the deployment process that was kept hold / paused resumes automatically.

    => Rollback the Deployment

    You can also roll back deployments to the previous versions. This is a critical feature allowing us to undo changes in deployment!!

    For example, Say there is a production bug deployed in the cluster, we can simply roll back the deployment to the previous version which is just a command away with no downtime until the bug is fixed!

    -> Understanding the internals first! To roll back to previous versions... Yes, there is someone helping us here!! It's the Deployment controller’s history function.

    Every deployment is tracked in the history.
Items.Author: ktnl

Items.Title: 不要使用 Console.log 来记录对象列表！ | Don't Use Console.log to Log a List of Objects!
Items.Description:
在调试 JavaScript 应用程序时， console.log()是登录浏览器控制台以获取对象或任何内容的值的最有价值的方法之一。

但是在显示对象列表时导航非常具有挑战性。

例如，让我们通过控制台记录这些数据。

 const data = [ { " _id " : " 628332c6b14a04d1708be802 " , " index " : 0 , " guid " : " 61ec3d19-f7f3-4083-8bef-b9042c881c79 " , " isActive " : true }, { " _id " : " 628332c61dc4ecd57ae4f6fd " , " index " : 1 , " guid " : " 49ea2d62-afec-4f62-9eae-0890a94462e3 " , " isActive " : true }, { " _id " : " 628332c6391c257b04b3c574 " , " index " : 2 , " guid " : " a7f2240b-5b9b-4786-8b9d-92307f313349 " , " isActive " : true }, { " _id " : " 628332c66e1d9b58e696e38f " , " index " : 3 , " guid " : " b52e0d56-28d8-418f-a30f-e431b7fcaa20 " , " isActive " : true }, { " _id " : " 628332c676d375dc108e0f28 " , " index " : 4 , " guid " : " e37e0f56-1c35-4f59-bb12-eda874be49fb " , " isActive " : false } ]

现在让我们使用console.table(data)代替。

数据更具可读性和可导航性。 您可以在此处了解有关console.table的更多信息。

使用bloggu.io发布的文章。 免费试用。



原文:

When debugging your JavaScript application, console.log() is one of the most valuable methods to log into the browser console for the value of an object or anything.

But it's pretty challenging to navigate when displaying a list of objects.

For example, let's console log this data.

const data = [ { "_id": "628332c6b14a04d1708be802", "index": 0, "guid": "61ec3d19-f7f3-4083-8bef-b9042c881c79", "isActive": true }, { "_id": "628332c61dc4ecd57ae4f6fd", "index": 1, "guid": "49ea2d62-afec-4f62-9eae-0890a94462e3", "isActive": true }, { "_id": "628332c6391c257b04b3c574", "index": 2, "guid": "a7f2240b-5b9b-4786-8b9d-92307f313349", "isActive": true }, { "_id": "628332c66e1d9b58e696e38f", "index": 3, "guid": "b52e0d56-28d8-418f-a30f-e431b7fcaa20", "isActive": true }, { "_id": "628332c676d375dc108e0f28", "index": 4, "guid": "e37e0f56-1c35-4f59-bb12-eda874be49fb", "isActive": false }
]

And now let's use console.table(data) instead.

The data is much more readable and navigable. You can learn more about console.table here.

Article posted using bloggu.io. Try it for free.
Items.Author: koladev

Items.Title: 纤细的组件 | Svelte Components
Items.Description:
简单来说，组件是网页的独立部分。 基本上，它们是创建网页的构建块，可以将其放在一起并输出到 dom 或浏览器以创建整个网站。

例如，您网页上的nav bar或footer可以是一个组件。 在 svelte kit 中，整个页面也由组件表示。 例如，假设您有一个 about 页面，那么为了表示该 about 页面，您还需要一个about component 。 一个 svelte 组件的命名必须是something.svelte ，例如对于一个 about 页面，它可以是about.svelte 。 这意味着.svelte扩展名被用于命名 svelte 组件。

在一个苗条的组件内部，我们基本上可以拥有 3 种不同的东西 -

  1. 我们通常在src folder中编写所有纤细的代码。 我们创建组件，然后将它们注入到 dom 中。 我们来自不同组件的所有脚本以及来自组件的所有样式都捆绑在一起，并在 svelte 中制作成一个文件。

     <script> let name = 'Arya'; </script> <main> <h1> hello {name}</h1> </main>

    上面的代码块解释了我们动态编写 svelte 的语法。

    将变量或值绑定到输入字段 -

     <input type = "text" bind:value = " " >

    为了将变量或值绑定到输入字段，我们所要做的就是添加和输入类型文本并添加绑定属性：并添加我们想要绑定到输入值的内容。

    这有助于我们实现的是，当输入的值发生变化时，变量也会相应地发生变化，反之亦然。



原文:

*What is a component? *

A component in simple words is a stand alone section of a webpage. Basically they are the building blocks to create a web page which can be put together and output to the dom or the browser to create a whole website.

For example the nav baror footer on your webpage can be a component. In svelte kit whole page is represented by component as well. Say for example you have an about page, then in order to represent that about page you would have an about component also. Naming of a svelte component has to be something.svelte, for example for an about page it can beabout.svelte. That means the .svelteextension is being used in naming svelte components.

Inside a svelte component we can essentially have 3 different things -

  1. an html template- which is the html page that is rendered to the browser.
  2. a script tag - where any interactive javascript which we inject in to the webpage goes in.
  3. a style tag- where any css to style the template goes in.

We usually write all of our svelte code inside the src folder. We create the components and later inject them into the dom. All of our scripts from our different components and all of our styling from the components are bundled together and made in to a single file in svelte.

<script>
let name = 'Arya';
</script>

<main>
<h1> hello {name}</h1>
</main>

The code block above explains the syntax in which we dynamically write svelte.

Binding variables or values to input fields -

<input type = "text" bind:value = " "  >

In order to bind a variable or value to input fields, all we have to do is add and input type text and add in a bind attribute : and add what we would like to bind to the value of the input.

What this help us achieve is that when the value of the input changes, then the variable changes accordingly and vice versa.
Items.Author: aryakris

Items.Title: K8s 对象 - 第 3 部分 [服务] | K8s Objects - Part 3 [Service]
Items.Description:
部署可确保所需数量的 Pod 在任何给定时间点以所需配置启动并运行。

但是，当添加新 Pod 时（由于缩放或版本更改）......我们知道 Pod 有一个 IP 可以访问，我们使用端口转发从外部访问它。

当 Pod 发生变化时，IP 也会随之变化。 鉴于此，使用 IP 经常更改的 Pod 维护您的应用程序是一项挑战。 为了确保您的应用程序与外界的无缝通信，K8s Service 来了！

由集群的一组 ipTable 规则组成的虚拟组件是 K8s 服务。

  • 它用于暴露 Pod，而不是与 Pod 对话，而最终只与服务对话。

  • 该服务负责路由流量和/或与 Pod 通信。

  • 将服务与您的 K8s 部署或 Replicaset 或 Pod 链接起来既简单又相同 - 像往常一样，只有匹配标签会为您执行此操作 :)

    • 服务类型

    集群IP

  • 节点端口
  • 负载均衡器
  • 外部名称

    • 默认情况下，K8s 创建一个 ClusterIP 类型的服务。 我们可以通过在服务配置文件的 spec.type 属性中指定类型来构建不同类型的服务。
    让我们一一探索！

    为了确保演示 LoadBalancer 类型的服务，我在 Azure 中创建了一个集群并在那里创建这些服务。

    集群IP

    • 在集群内部 IP 上公开集群内的服务。 应用程序可以使用 ClusterIP 在内部与其他应用程序进行交互。

    • 从集群外部无法访问该服务。

    • 这是默认的服务类型。

    演示时间！

    这个配置文件创建了一个管理三个 nginx Pod 的部署，并将它们暴露在 ClusterIP 服务下。

    集群中的其他应用程序可以使用公开的 InternalIP 10.0.248.71访问这些 Pod。

    节点端口

    • 将您的服务暴露在集群之外。 这会在静态端口上创建 Pod 到其托管节点的映射。 因此，该服务可在 NodeIP:NodePort 上访问。

    • 通常取值在 30000 - 32767 之间。

    演示时间！

    应用此配置，会产生另外三个 Pod。

    这些 Pod 现在通过 Node Port 服务在 :30003 处向外界公开（您在配置文件中提到了 30003，否则 K8s 从允许的范围内随机选择。）

    负载均衡器

    • 这会在 AWS、GCP、Azure 等各种云提供商中创建负载均衡器，并将我们的应用程序暴露给 Internet。

    • 云提供商将提供一种将流量路由到服务的机制。

    演示时间！

    应用配置，创建通过负载均衡器公开的 Pod。

    现在，全世界都可以通过 20.198.164.24 访问该应用程序。 外部名称

    • 将服务映射到 externalName 字段的内容。 在集群中访问您的服务会重定向到您提供的 externalName。

    • 它不是任何典型的选择器标签。 相反，它附加到外部服务器的 CNAME。

    这将创建一个服务，当在 my-service.default.svc.cluster.local 访问时，它会重定向到 my.service.com 的内容。

    希望这能介绍 Service K8s 对象。 下一篇博客见。

    快乐学习！



    原文:

    Deployment ensures that the desired number of Pods are up and running with the desired configuration at any given point of time.

    But, when a new Pod is added (due to scaling or version changes)... We know that Pod has an IP to reach, and we use port forwarding to reach it from the outside world.

    When the Pod changes the IP also changes along with it. Given this, maintaining your application with Pods whose IPs can frequently change is a challenge. In order to ensure the seamless communication of your application with the outside world, the K8s Service comes as the saviour!

    The K8s Service

    • The virtual component that consists of the set of ipTable rules for the cluster is the K8s Service.

    • It is used to expose Pods, instead of talking to the pods you end up talking with just the service.

    • The service takes the responsibility of routing the traffic and/or communicating with the Pods.

    • Linking the service with your K8s Deployment or Replicaset or Pod is simple and same - As usual just the match labels does this for you :)

    Types of Services

    There are four types of K8s Services.

    1. ClusterIP
    2. NodePort
    3. LoadBalancer
    4. ExternalName

    By default, K8s creates a ClusterIP type of service. We can build different kinds of services by specifying the type in a spec.type property of the service configuration file.
    Let's explore them one by one!

    To ensure demoing the LoadBalancer type of service, I have created a Cluster in Azure and creating these services there.

    ClusterIP

    • Exposes your service within your cluster on a cluster-internal IP. Applications can interact with other applications internally using the ClusterIP.

    • The Service is unreachable from outside the cluster.

    • This is the default ServiceType.

    Demo time!

    clusterIp-service-demo.yml

    This configuration file creates a deployment managing three nginx Pods and exposes them under the ClusterIP service.

    These Pods can be accessed using the exposed InternalIP 10.0.248.71 by other applications within the cluster.

    NodePort

    • Exposes your service outside your cluster. This creates a mapping of Pods to it's hosting node on a static port. Thus the service is accessible at the NodeIP:NodePort.

    • NodeIP is the IP address of your node and NodePort is the port which you decide to expose the service at. Usually something taken between 30000 - 32767.

    Demo Time!

    Applying this configuration, results in three more Pods.

    These Pods are now exposed through Node Port service to the outside world at :30003 (30003 as you have mentioned in configuration file, else K8s randomly picks from the allowed range.)

    LoadBalancer

    • This creates load balancers in various Cloud providers like AWS, GCP, Azure, etc., and exposes our application to the Internet.

    • The Cloud provider will provide a mechanism for routing the traffic to the services.

    Demo Time!

    Applying the configuration, creates Pods exposed via loadbalancer.

    Now, the application is accessible at 20.198.164.24 to the world. (Don't try to access, I will delete the service shortly to save my penny :P)

    ExternalName

    • Maps the Service to the contents of the externalName field. Accessing your service within your cluster redirects to the externalName you have provided.

    • It is not to any typical selector labels. Rather it is attached to the CNAME of the external server.

    This creates a service which when accessed at my-service.default.svc.cluster.local redirects to the content of my.service.com.

    Hope this gives an introduction to Service K8s object. See you in the next blog.

    Happy learning!
Items.Author: ktnl

Items.Title: #15：使用 Azure 工具进行开发！ | #15: Develop with Azure Tools!
Items.Description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA的Week 3, Day 1天！

我们刚刚在#30DaysOfSWA 上跨过了一半！ 我们在第 1 周介绍了核心概念，并在第 2 周探讨了使用示例。今天我们开始第 3 周，重点是开发人员工具！ 让我们潜入吧！

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 开发者体验是什么样的？
  • 探索：Azure CLI
  • 探索：Azure 门户
  • 探索：Azure Core Functions 工具
  • 练习：探索 SWA 的 Azure 门户和 CLI 文档。

开发者体验

当我们考虑构建现代 Web 应用程序时，我们也在考虑我们的开发人员体验。 根据一个定义，开发人员体验是指我们作为开发人员执行所需任务以实现预期结果的难易程度。

在应用程序开发的情况下，任务通常涉及：

  • 从源代码构建项目（在创建代码库之后）
  • 测试项目行为（以验证是否符合要求）
  • 部署项目（到登台或生产环境）
  • 调试项目（修复问题、审计性能等）

拥有正确的开发人员工具可以使高效的开发人员工作流程和痛苦的工作流程完全不同。 本周，我们将了解许多可用的工具和服务，它们使 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序开发人员体验更加无缝。

  • 使用 Azure 的核心工具是什么？
  • 剧作家 | 我们如何为我们的应用程序实施端到端测试？
  • 集成/光盘| 我们如何自动化构建/部署工作流程？
  • 在今天的博文中，我们将介绍使现代 Web 应用程序开发人员能够轻松在 Azure 上构建静态 Web 应用程序的主要工具：

    我们将在本周晚些时候更详细地介绍 Azure SWA CLI 和 VS Code 扩展。

    刚接触 Azure？

    我们假设您熟悉资源、资源组、订阅和服务等云计算概念。 如果没有，那也没关系。 现在是绕道而行探索Azure 基础知识的好时机 - 这是一个由 6 部分组成的学习系列，将带你了解 Azure 上可用的核心概念、服务、解决方案、安全功能、身份和治理方面以及成本管理功能。

    如果您是 Azure 的 JavaScript 或 Node.js 开发人员，请将这些链接添加为书签：

    现在你已经熟悉了 Azure 的核心概念，让我们来谈谈如何在 Azure 上创建和管理你的云资源。 熟悉 Azure CLI 命令的最简单方法是在浏览器的 Azure Cloud Shell 中安装和运行它- 无需本地安装。 按照这些说明在您的开发环境中本地安装它。 通过测试核心az命令来验证您的安装。

    以下是受支持服务的列表以及每个服务的快速入门文档的链接。 作为 Web 开发人员，您可能会发现与Web 相关的服务子集值得探索 - 从应用服务和 API 管理到 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序的所有内容。

    使用以下命令获取有关此上下文中支持的命令和选项的交互式帮助。

    使用 CLI，您可以管理应用程序设置、创建和删除静态应用程序、管理静态应用程序的环境和相关功能、监督与应用程序相关的身份、用户和机密等信息。

    如果您更喜欢基于 Web 的统一控制台来管理您的 Azure 资源交互， Azure 门户提供了Azure CLI 的图形用户界面替代方案。 该门户专为持续可用性而设计。 这是 Azure 门户布局的快照。 浏览以下文档以更深入地了解其功能：

    然后，尝试使用 Azure 门户构建您的第一个静态 Web 应用程序，以亲身体验从创建到部署的开发人员工作流程。

    在我们之前关于启用 API 的 SWA的文章中了解更多相关信息，其中我们讨论了托管函数（由 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序创建和管理）和自带函数（独立创建和管理）选项，用于向静态 Web 添加无服务器 API 支持应用。

    安装后，使用以下命令获取有关此工具支持的命令和选项的交互式帮助：

    您应该会看到类似这样的内容（为清楚起见，一些输出被缩写）让您了解可以从此 CLI 执行的主要操作。

     Azure Functions Core Tools Core Tools Version: 4.0.3971 ... Function Runtime Version: 4.0.1.16815 Usage: func [context] [context] <action> [-/--options] Contexts: azure Commands to log in to Azure and manage resources durable Commands for working with Durable Functions extensions Commands for installing extensions function Commands for creating and running functions locally host Commands for running the Functions host locally kubernetes Commands for working with Kubernetes and Azure Functions settings Commands for managing environment settings for the local Functions host templates Commands for listing available function templates Actions: start Launches the functions runtime host --port [-p] Local port to listen on. Default: 7071 .. ..

    浏览文档以了解如何：

    本周晚些时候，我们将深入探讨Azure 静态 Web 应用程序 CLI ，它是 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序的本地开发命令行工具，可与 Azure Functions 核心工具无缝协作，为开发人员提供统一的创建和测试启用 API 的静态 Web 应用程序项目。

    但是，如果您更喜欢基于 IDE 的方法怎么办？ 在本周晚些时候的一篇文章中，我们将深入探讨您可以使用的关键 Visual Studio 代码扩展，以增强您的开发人员体验，从代码到云，就在 IDE 中。 现在，我将为您提供以下资源：

    这是Microsoft当前在Azure类别中发布的扩展快照，按趋势指标排序。 您可以看到此处列出的Azure 静态 Web 应用程序扩展——我们将在本周的最后一篇文章中更详细地介绍它！

    锻炼

    哇——我们今天报道了很多！！ 但不必担心必须记住所有这些。 目标是提高人们对使用哪种工具、何时使用以及为什么使用的认识。 你今天的练习：

    • 为 Azure 静态 Web 应用选择Azure 门户或Azure CLI文档，并熟悉可用的许多命令、设置和选项。

      • 尝试该选项的默认快速入门 - 然后使用已部署的静态 Web 应用程序探索其他命令，以真正了解其工作原理。

    资源



原文:

Welcome to Week 3, Day 1 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

We just crossed the halfway mark on #30DaysOfSWA! We covered core concepts in Week 1, and explored usage examples in Week 2. Today we kick off week 3 with a focus on Developer Tools! Let's dive in!

What We'll Cover

  • What does the Developer Experience look like?
  • Explore: Azure CLI
  • Explore: Azure Portal
  • Explore: Azure Core Functions Tools
  • Exercise: Explore Azure Portal & CLI docs for SWA.

The Developer Experience

When we think about building a modern web application, we're also thinking about our developer experience. By one definition, developer experience refers to how easy or difficult it is for us, as developers, to perform the required tasks to implement the desired outcome.

In the case of application development, the tasks often involve:

  • Building the project from source (after creating the codebase)
  • Testing project behaviors (to validate compliance with requirements)
  • Deploying the project (to staging or production environments)
  • Debugging the project (to fix issues, audit performance etc.)

Having the right developer tools can make all the difference between a productive developer workflow and a painful one. This week, we'll look at the many tools and services available, that make the Azure Static Web Apps Developer Experience more seamless.

  • Azure Dev Tools | What are the core tools for working with Azure?
  • Playwright | How can we implement end-to-end testing for our app?
  • CI/CD| How can we automate build/deploy workflows?
  • Edge DevTools How can we debug and profile apps in the browser?
  • SWA CLI How can we simplify local development for static web apps?
  • Visual Studio Code How can we supercharge our IDE-driven experience?

In today's post we'll look at the main tools that make it easy for modern web application developers to build static web apps on Azure:

We'll coverAzure SWA CLI and VS Code extensions in greater detail later this week.

New to Azure?

We assume you are familiar with cloud computing concepts like resources, resource groups, subscriptions and services. If not, that's okay. Now's a good time to take a detour and explore Azure Fundamentals - a 6-part learning series that will take you through the core concepts, services, solutions, security features, identity & governance aspects, and cost management features available on Azure.

If you're a JavaScript or Node.js developer new to Azure, bookmark these links:

Azure CLI

Now that you're familiar with core Azure concepts, let's talk about how you can create and manage your cloud resources on Azure. The Azure CLI is a cross-platform command-line tool that connects you securely to Azure, to execute commands on Azure resources interactively (or via scripts) from a terminal.

The easiest way to familiarize yourself with the Azure CLI commands is to install and run it in Azure Cloud Shell in your browser - no local installation needed. Follow these instructions to then install it locally in your development environment. Validate your install by testing out the core az command.

There Azure CLI can manage most Azure Services out of the box. Here is list of supportred services along with links to quickstart documentation for each. As web developers, you may find the web-related subset of services worth exploring - everything from App Service and API Management, to Azure Static Web Apps.

Use the following command to get interactive help with supported commands and options in this context.

With the CLI you can manage app settings, create and delete static apps, manage the static app's environment and associated functions, oversee information related to identity, users and secrets associated with the app, and more.

Azure Portal

If you prefer a unified web-based console for managing your Azure resource interactions, the Azure Portal provides a graphical user interface alternative to the Azure CLI. The portal is designed for continuous availability. Here's a snapshot of what the Azure Portal layout looks like. Browse the following documents to get a deeper dive into its capabilities:

Then, try to build your first static web app using the Azure Portal to get hands-on experience with the developer workflow from creation to deployment.

Azure Functions Core Tools

Azure Static Web Apps uses Azure Functions to support serverless API capability for the app. Learn more about this in our earlier post on API-enabling SWA where we talked about Managed Functions (created and managed by Azure Static Web Apps) and Bring Your Own Functions (independently created and managed) options for adding serverless API support to your static web application.

The Azure Functions Core Tools - aka func - supports local development with a command-line tool to create, test, and even deploy, your functions to Azure. Once installed, use the following to get interactive help with the supported commands and options for this tool:

You should see something like this (some output abbreviated for clarity) giving you a sense of the main operations you can perform from this CLI.

Azure Functions Core Tools
Core Tools Version:       4.0.3971 ...
Function Runtime Version: 4.0.1.16815

Usage: func [context] [context] <action> [-/--options]

Contexts:
azure       Commands to log in to Azure and manage resources
durable     Commands for working with Durable Functions
extensions  Commands for installing extensions
function    Commands for creating and running functions locally
host        Commands for running the Functions host locally
kubernetes  Commands for working with Kubernetes and Azure Functions
settings    Commands for managing environment settings for the local Functions host
templates   Commands for listing available function templates

Actions: 
start   Launches the functions runtime host  
    --port [-p]     Local port to listen on. 
                    Default: 7071
..
..

Explore the documentation to learn how you can:

Later this week, we'll do a deep-dive into the Azure Static Web Apps CLI - a local development commandline tool for Azure Static Web Apps, that works seamlessly with the Azure Functions Core Tool, to deliver an unified developer experience for creating and testing your API-enabled Static Web Apps projects.

The combination of Azure CLI (to manage Azure resources from command line), Azure Functions Core Tools (to manage Azure Functions local development and deploy) and Azure Static Web Apps CLI (to manage SWA local development and deploy) provides a seamless command-line driven developer experience for web developers on Azure.

Azure Extensions

But what if you prefer an IDE-based approach? Visual Studio Code has you covered. In a post Later this week, we'll look in depth at the key Visual Studio Code Extensions you can use, to supercharge your developer experience from code to cloud, right inside the IDE. For now, I'll leave you with these resources:

Here is a snapshot of extensions currently published by Microsoft in the Azure category, sorted by Trending metrics. You can see the Azure Static Web Apps Extension listed here -- we'll cover that in more detail in the final post of this week!

Exercise

Wow - we covered a lot today!! But don't worry about having to remember all of it. The goal was to raise awareness of which tool to use, when, and why. Your exercise today:

  • pick either Azure Portal or Azure CLI documentation for Azure Static Web Apps, and familiarize yourself with the many commands, settings and options available.

    • Try the default quickstart for that option - then explore other commands with the deployed Static Web App to get a real sense of how this works.

Items.Title: dbForge 用于 SQL Server 的 SQL Documenter 初探 | A first glance at dbForge’s SQL Documenter for SQL Server
Items.Description:
亚历杭德罗·科巴尔

发表于因此，10 多年来，我一直是“普通”的 SQL Server 数据库管理员。 如今，有这么多可用的 3rd 方工具，“做出正确的选择”有点困难。 无论如何，我继续四处搜索以从 Google 先生那里获得任何建议，并且我有机地发现了 Devart 的 SQL 工具。

当您查看他们的网站以快速了解该工具提供的功能时，真是太棒了！ 在套件中可用的所有工具中，有一个引起了我的初步注意，它是 SQL Server 的 SQL Documenter。 为什么？ 嗯，因为在这10多年的行业里，我在很多地方工作过，我从来没有见过一个人做这样的事情； 我知道这在任何软件开发实践中都非常重要，但是当您在 IT 世界的这段旅程中经历了一定的里程时，这似乎更像是一个神话。 这就是我试一试的原因，并想与您分享一些见解，说明为什么这个特定工具可以使您（明星 SQL Server DBA）在竞争中脱颖而出； 我什至考虑在以后的文章中介绍其余的工具，敬请期待！

初步考虑
由于这是我第一次尝试其中一个产品，我将安装整个套件来讨论 SQL Documenter，并让其余的产品为下一轮文章做好准备。

安装我选择了专业试用选项，你知道，因为我们是专业人士，没错！

您可以从此处下载捆绑包

我想在这一点上提到这将是一个 Windows 唯一的东西有点明显，但这不应该是一个值得一试的展示。

一旦你打开安装程序，这就是你将得到的。

现在，一旦你到达这个屏幕，你就可以挑选你想要的任何东西（我当然同意了；））。

在等待安装程序完成它的事情之后，你应该很高兴。

如果你看一下你的桌面，你会看到每个工具的一堆快捷方式：
我告诉过你我是一个“普通”的 SQL Server DBA ;)

一旦您打开 dbForge Documenter，您将看到以下屏幕，由于这是我们第一次与之交互，我们别无选择，只能单击“新建”：

首先要做的是选择一个 SQL Server 实例连接，所以我们必须通过单击“管理”按钮来创建一个：

在下一个屏幕中，我们单击“新建”：

在此屏幕中，您将看到用于填写连接字符串、服务器名称、身份验证类型、数据库以及用户和密码（如有必要）的经典字段。 在底部，您将看到一个下拉列表，其中包含一些用于对您正在创建的连接进行分类的选项：

但这还不是全部，如果您单击下拉列表旁边的三点按钮，您可以选择创建自己的类别并随心所欲地进行：

在“高级”选项卡中，您将有面板为您的连接字符串指定任何其他参数，例如“ApplicationIntent=ReadOnly”触发可用性组的只读路由，或者如果您想要的是“加密=真”使用加密连接，仅举几例：

如果您正确输入所有内容，您将看到连接成功的确认信息：

有了这个，让我们开始当晚的主菜：在这个屏幕中，您将看到当前位于您的环境中的所有数据库，如果您单击其中一个，您将在屏幕左侧看到一个列表所选数据库的属性和切换：

我知道这篇文章的主题是指“记录器”的东西，到目前为止还没有类似的东西被证明过……直到现在：如果我们勾选主数据库的复选框，“生成……”按钮之前是灰色的-out 将可用，所以让我们单击它。

您将看到 3 个不同的选项来生成您的文档：HTML、PDF 和 Markdown。 对于这个演示，我使用了 HTML，因为它显然是交互式的，而且检查起来更有趣。

生成文档后，您将在打开它后立即看到以下内容：
您会注意到它与上面显示的屏幕具有相似的氛围，当然无需过多关注水印文本（因为我们使用的是评估许可证）。

这里没有什么太花哨的，只是对数据库及其所有属性的描述。

现在让我们尝试使用用户数据库来看看我们得到了什么：通过展开可用的表并单击一个，我们可以看到它为我们提供了非常有价值的信息。 在单个视图中，您可以查看索引信息、用于创建表的 SQL 脚本以及表是否存在任何依赖关系。



用例• DBA 可以在部署特定软件版本之前使用此工具生成所需数据库对象的文档，作为在报告异常情况时获得比较点的一种手段。 想象一下，您决定使用 bcp 备份数据，而不是常规备份策略，然后您将拥有创建表的脚本，然后使用 bcp 输出文件加载数据。 我知道这听起来很遥远，但并非完全不可能。 最后的想法• 这个工具可以让我在我工作过的所有公司的许多 SQL Server 实例上更顺畅。 正如我最初提到的，我在以前去过的任何地方都从未见过这样的事情，而且我强烈认为，现在开始实施我们一直听到的那些最佳实践永远不会太晚，但到目前为止，除了烟雾什么都没有。

特别是对于 SQL Server 数据库管理员团队，根据我的经验（我也猜想统计上的），他们相当小，没有太多时间专注于像这样的重要事情。



原文:

Alejandro Cobar

Posted on May 17

So for 10+ years I’ve been a “vanilla” SQL Server Database Administrator. By “vanilla” I mean that I’ve always worked only with the standard out-of-the-box tools for SQL Server (e.g. SQL Server Management Studio, and a handful of custom PowerShell scripts to automate things for me).

Nowadays, with so many 3rd party tools available, it’s a bit hard to “make the right choice”. Regardless, I went on and searched around to get any recommendations from Mr. Google, and I organically discovered SQL Tools from Devart.

When you take a look at their website to get a quick glimpse at what the tool offers, it’s amazing! Out of all the tools available within the suite, one got my initial attention, and it’s the SQL Documenter for SQL Server. Why? Well, because in those 10+ years in the industry, I’ve worked in many places, and I’ve never seen a single person that does such thing; I know that it is something super important within any software development practice, but it seems more like a myth when you have certain miles traveled through this journey in the IT world. That’s why I gave it a shot and want to share with you some insights of why this particular tool can differentiate you, the star SQL Server DBA, from the competition; I’m even thinking of covering the rest of the tools in future articles, so stay tuned!

Initial consideration
Since this is my very first time giving a go to one of the products, I will be installing the whole suite to talk about SQL Documenter and have the rest of them ready for the next rounds of articles.

Installation I went with the Professional Trial option, you know, because we are pros right!

You can download the bundle from here

I guess at this point it’s a bit obvious to mention that this will be a Windows only thing, but that shouldn’t be a showstopper to give it a worthy shot.

As soon as you open the installer, this is what you’ll get.

Now, once you reach this screen, you can cherry-pick whatever you want (I went with all of course ;)).

After waiting for the installer to do its thing, you should be good to go.

If you take a look at your desktop, you’ll see a bunch of shortcuts for each tool:
*See those PowerShell scripts in there? I told you I was a “vanilla” SQL Server DBA ;)

As soon as you open dbForge Documenter, you’ll see the following screen, and since it’s the very first time we’re interacting with it, we’re left with no choice but to click on “New”:

The first thing to do is pick a SQL Server instance connection, so we’re going to have to create one by clicking the “Manage” button:

In the next screen we click on “New”:

In this screen you’ll see your classic fields to fill for a connection string, the server name, authentication type, database, and user & password if necessary. At the bottom you’ll see a dropdown with some options to categorize the connection you are creating:

But that’s not all, if you click on the three-dot button next to the dropdown, you’ll have the option to create your own category and go as wild as you like:

In the “Advanced” tab you’ll have the panel to specify any additional parameters for your connection string, for instance “ApplicationIntent=ReadOnly” to trigger read-only routing for an AvailabilityGroup, or “encrypt=true” if what you want is to work with an encrypted connection, just to name a few:

If you enter everything correctly, then you’ll see a confirmation that the connection succeeded:

With that, let’s get to the main dish of the evening: In this screen you’ll see all the databases currently sitting within your environment, and if you click one of them, you’ll see in the left section of the screen a list of properties and toggles for the selected database:

I know that the topic of this article refers to a “documenter” thing, and so far nothing like that has been demonstrated… until right now: If we tick the checkbox for the master database, the “Generate…” button that was previously greyed-out will be made available, so let’s click it.

You will be presented with 3 different options to generate your documentation: HTML, PDF and Markdown. For this demonstration, I went with HTML, since it’ll obviously be interactive and more fun to check around.

After generating the document, you’ll see the following, right after opening it:
You will notice that it has a similar vibe as the screens presented above, without caring too much about the watermark text of course (since we’re using an evaluation license).

Nothing too fancy here, just the description of the database along with all its properties.

Now let’s try with a user database to see what we get: By expanding the available tables and clicking on one, we can see that it gives us extremely valuable information. Within a single view you can take a look at indexes information, the SQL script that is used to create the table, and if there are any dependencies that the table has.



Use cases • A DBA could use this tool to generate the documentation of the desired database objects, prior to the deployment of a specific software release, as a means to have a comparison point in case something weird is reported. • It is a great way to have at least a backup of the definition of the database objects. Imagine that, instead of a regular backup strategy, you decide to backup data using bcp, then with this you’d have the scripts to create the tables and then the bcp output files to load the data. I know that it sounds like a long shot, but not a totally impossible one. • If you have multiple SQL Server instances under your umbrella, then this is a magnificent tool, not only to document all of them properly, but to use it as introductory material for new hires coming to your team.

• With this documentation, a DBA could easily spot users/logins, in a Production environment, that have high privileges assigned and shouldn’t.

Final thoughts • This tool would’ve smoothen my way around many SQL Server instances across all the companies that I worked at. As I mentioned initially, I have never seen anything like this in any of the places I’ve previously been, and I’m strongly thinking that it’s never too late to start with the implementation of those best practices that we’ve always heard, but have been nothing but smoke so far.

• I haven’t gone over the rest of the tools within the suite yet, but with what I’ve seen so far, I personally think that the investment that any company could make on this product is totally worth it. Especially for teams of SQL Server Database Administrators that, in my experience (and I’d guess that statistically too), are rather small and don’t have much time in their hands to focus on important stuff like this.
Items.Author: alejandrocobar

Items.Title: 如何聘请 Python 开发人员：招聘开发人员的技巧 | How to Hire a Python Developer: Tips for hiring Developers
Items.Description:
发表于无论您是想开始您的第一个 Python 开发项目，还是有一个需要 Python 开发人员的成熟企业，找到合适的人选都具有挑战性。 如果您错误地过分关注他们的技术技能而忽略了其他重要因素，例如沟通和动力，那么您最终可能会与不适合您的团队文化或产生低于标准的结果的人一起工作。 那么如何找到优秀的 Python 开发人员呢？ 这里有 10 个技巧可以帮助您开始招聘 Python 开发人员，这将提高您的生产力并确保您的产品或服务符合您的质量标准。

什么是 Python？

在雇用任何开发人员之前，您必须确切地知道他们将负责什么。 如果您不精通编码语言，或者您自己不是活跃的开发人员，请从熟悉 Python 开始。 它可用于许多行业，尤其​​受 Betterment 和 Robinhood 等金融科技公司的欢迎。

一些公司甚至在其营销部门使用它来收集有关客户人口统计和购买模式的大数据集。 此外，还有很多在线教程可以让学习变得简单。 如果您想尝试编码，有很多方法可以学习一些基础知识！ 查看 CodeCademy 或 Treehouse，了解有关编程如何工作以及应用程序可以做什么的大量交互式课程。

在雇用开发人员之前您需要了解的内容

关于开发人员需要哪些技能以及雇用具有相关经验的人的重要性有很多文章和讨论。 但是，当您为 Python 开发人员招聘时，很容易忽略一些重要的事情。 如果您不熟悉组建工程团队，那么肯定有一些陷阱可以通过了解它们是什么来避免。

这篇文章将帮助您了解在审查潜在员工时应该提出哪些问题。 找到优秀的开发人员已经够难的了。 找到懂 Python 的人就更难了！ 如果您正在寻找以前从事过 Python 项目的人，那么在面试时要问他们以下五个问题：

我如何才能雇佣最好的 Python 开发人员？

知道如何雇佣 Python 开发人员可能很棘手。 因为您的团队将从事复杂的项目，所以您需要一个注重细节并能够独立工作的人。 也就是说，了解如何聘用一般的开发人员也很重要。

在雇用任何员工或承包商时，您需要考虑几件事：他们是否在需要时可用？ 他们能按时完成吗？ 他们能接受别人的指导和批评吗？ 这里有 10 个技巧，可以帮助您聘请熟练且值得信赖的 Python 开发人员。

为什么我应该在下一个项目中使用 Python？

如果你正在寻找一个易于阅读和遵循的关于如何雇佣 python 开发人员的指南，那么你很幸运。 以下是一些关于如何找到优秀软件工程师和开发人员的技巧，无论他们的经验水平如何。

如果您对以符合您预算的价格从知名公司招聘开发人员有任何疑问，请立即联系我们！ 我们可以提供任何帮助，从为您的工作职位寻找合适的人一直到价格谈判。 让我们开始吧！...

谁是这种语言背后的人？

自发布以来，Python 已发展成为当今最流行和最通用的编程语言之一——用于游戏 (Minecraft)、Web 应用程序 (YouTube) 和科学应用程序 (SETI)。

由于它的受欢迎程度和学习的容易程度（以及其他原因），学习如何聘请 Python 开发人员可能是您发展业务时最好的投资之一。 但是你从哪里开始呢？ 有哪些重要因素将决定您的项目是成功还是失败？ 继续阅读！

是否需要现有的技能组合？

每家公司都是不同的，近年来招聘实践已经取得了长足的进步，因此了解贵公司当前的招聘流程非常重要。 如果您在人力资源部门工作，您可能已经对面试潜在员工时需要发生的事情有所了解。

如果没有，请花一些时间与参与招聘的同事交谈。 询问他们如何进行采访，他们提出了哪些问题，以及为什么在他们的过程中会出现某些问题。

与现有系统的兼容性如何？

一旦你确定了你的前三四名候选人，你可以做一些事情来保持你的决策过程顺利进行。

理想情况下，有一些您需要每个候选人满足的既定标准。 这将有助于在流程的早期淘汰不合格的候选人，并将您的注意力集中在满足您所有所需资格的人身上。 在安排面试时，请确保他们相距不远。

您最不希望的事情是让他们中的一些或全部失败，因为面试与另一家公司的面试重叠。 如果确实有人不得不在短时间内取消，请询问他们是否可以尽快重新安排 - 如果他们的下一个预定时段距离取消日期还有几周，请谨慎行事。

我能否为我的团队找到任何训练有素的开发人员？

在评估您的团队中是否有人有资格招聘时，重要的是要考虑您到底在寻找什么。

例如，如果您需要一位熟悉非常特殊的编程语言（例如 Clojure）的专家开发人员，那么可能很难找到具有这些资格的人。

因此，请考虑聘请在更主流语言方面具有强大技能并且可以随着时间的推移发展他们的技能的人，而不是急于聘请一个经验不足的人，而他们恰好知道你想要什么。 积累技术债务是没有人想要但如果我们提前计划很容易避免的事情之一。

我应该如何开始面试过程？

首先，确定你的必备品和好品。 你的理想候选人绝对需要什么？ 您是否需要他们能够并愿意搬迁？ 他们需要多少年的经验？ 这是重要的一步，因为您不想浪费时间采访不符合您所有标准的人。

其次，确保参与面试潜在候选人的每个人都有明确的职位描述。 例如，如果您的团队中的两名成员（不包括您自己）各进行 30% 的空缺职位面试，那么请确保双方都了解他们在面试中应该寻找的技能和素质。

聘请开发人员后的下一步是什么？

一旦您找到了您的开发人员并成功加入他们，就该开始了。 虽然您可能已经准备好立即开始编码，但您的新员工需要一些时间来调整。 因此，给他们至少两周的时间进行适当的入职培训，然后在此期间安排与他们定期签到。

每周或每两周安排一个小时，并借此机会让他们提出有关公司程序的问题，回顾他们那一周的目标等。此外，确保您讨论他们如何在组织内朝着他们的期望前进并在需要时提供帮助。

一起审查项目规范； 询问您的新员工他们对规范有什么问题，可以让您了解工作流程中可能出现的混乱。

使用 Python 接触热门技术

例如，如果您希望您的初创公司在当今市场中保持最新并与时俱进，那么您的技术团队必须紧跟重要的创新趋势，这一点至关重要。

有什么比使用像 python 这样的尖端技术更好的方法来确保你的初创公司处于领先地位？ 作为一种开源编程语言，新开发人员有许多简单的方法可以开始使用 python — 并获得宝贵的经验来开发贵公司的下一个大型应用程序！ 如果您对雇用 python 开发人员是否适合您或哪种流程最适合成长中的技术团队有任何疑问，我们很乐意提供帮助。

JetBrains TeamCity - JetBrains TeamCity - May 10 5月10日



原文:

It support

Posted on May 17

Whether you’re looking to start your first Python development project or you have an established business that needs a Python developer, finding the right fit can be challenging. If you make the mistake of focusing too much on their technical skills and ignore other important factors, such as communication and motivation, you could end up working with someone who doesn’t jive well with your team culture or produces subpar results. So how do you find a great Python developer? Here are 10 tips to help you get started hiring Python developers that will boost your productivity and ensure that your product or service meets your quality standards.

What Is Python?

Before hiring any developer, it’s essential that you know exactly what it is they’ll be responsible for. If you aren’t well versed in coding languages, or aren’t an active developer yourself, start by getting familiar with Python. It can be used across many industries and is particularly popular with financial technology companies like Betterment and Robinhood.

Some companies even use it in their marketing departments to collect big data sets on customer demographics and purchase patterns. Plus, there are plenty of online tutorials that make learning simple. There are plenty of ways to pick up some basics if you want to give coding a try! Check out CodeCademy or Treehouse for tons of interactive courses on how programming works and what applications can do.

What you need to know before hiring a developer

There are plenty of articles and discussions about what skills you need in developers and how important hiring someone with relevant experience is. But it’s easy to overlook some important things when you’re hiring for a Python developer. If you're new to building an engineering team, there are definitely some pitfalls that you can avoid by knowing what they are.

This post will help give you an idea of what questions should be coming up when vetting a potential employee. Finding great developers is hard enough. finding them who know Python is even harder! If you’re looking for someone who has worked on Python projects before, here are five questions to ask them during an interview:

1) Can we talk about previous work experiences? 2) What projects did your work on? 3) What are the key features of Python? 4) How is memory managed in Python?

5) What Extra you Bring by Joining our Python team?

How can I hire the best Python developers?

Knowing how to hire Python developers can be tricky. Because your team will be working on complex projects, you need someone who is detail-oriented and able to work independently. That said, knowing how to hire a developer in general is also important.

When hiring any employee or contractor, there are several things you need to consider: Are they available when needed? Do they meet deadlines? Can they take direction and criticism from others? Here are 10 tips for hiring a skilled and trustworthy Python developer.

Why should I use Python for my next project?

If you’re looking for an easy-to-read and follow guide on how to hire python developers, then you’re in luck. Below are some tips on what it takes to find great software engineers and developers, no matter their experience level.

And if you have any more questions regarding hiring developers from reputable companies at prices that will fit your budget, feel free to contact us today! We can help with anything, from finding the right people for your job positions all the way down to pricing negotiation. Let’s get started!...

Who Are The People Behind This Language?

Python was created by Guido van Rossum in 1991, who based it on ABC language. The name Python is derived from Monty Python's Flying Circus, but in reality, Guido had already considered naming it after a favorite drink of his - American Pilsner. Since its release, Python has evolved into one of the most popular and most versatile programming languages today - used for games (Minecraft), web applications (YouTube) and science applications (SETI).

Because of its popularity and because of how easy it is to learn (among other reasons), learning how to hire python developer can be one of your best investments as you grow your business. But where do you begin? What are some important factors that will determine if your project succeeds or fails? Keep reading!

Is There A Need For An Existing Skill Set?

Every company is different and hiring practices have come a long way in recent years, so it’s important to know your company’s current hiring process. If you’re working in HR, you might already have some sense of what needs to happen when interviewing potential employees.

If not, spend some time talking with colleagues who are involved in recruiting. Ask them how they conduct interviews, what questions they ask and why certain questions came up during their process.

What About Compatibility With Existing Systems?

Once you’ve determined your top three or four candidates, there are a few things you can do to keep your decision-making process running smoothly.

Ideally, have some set criteria you need each candidate to meet. This will help weed out unqualified candidates early in the process and focus your attention on those who meet all of your desired qualifications. When scheduling interviews, make sure they’re not too far apart.

The last thing you want is for some or all of them to fall through because an interview overlapped with another at another company. If it does happen that someone has to cancel at short notice, ask if they could reschedule as soon as possible—and then proceed with caution if their next scheduled slot is several weeks away from their cancellation date.

Will I Be Able To Find Any Trained Developers For My Team?

When evaluating whether you have people on your team that are qualified for hiring, it’s important to consider what exactly you’re looking for.

For example, if you need an expert developer who is familiar with an extremely niche programming language (such as Clojure), it might be hard to find someone with those exact qualifications.

As such, consider bringing on someone who has strong skills in more mainstream languages and can develop their skill set over time rather than rushing out and hiring a less experienced person who just happens to know exactly what you want. Building up technical debt is one of those things that no one wants but is easily avoidable if we plan ahead.

How Should I Begin The Interview Process?

First, identify your must haves and nice to haves. What are you absolutely going to need from your ideal candidate? Do you need them to be able and willing to relocate? How many years of experience do they need? This is an important step because you don't want to waste time interviewing people who won't meet all of your criteria.

Second, make sure that everyone who will be involved in interviewing potential candidates has clearly defined job descriptions. For example, if two members of your team (not counting yourself) are each conducting 30% of interviews for open positions, then be sure that both individuals understand what skills and qualities they should be looking for during those interviews.

What Next After Hiring A Developer?

Once you’ve found your developer and successfully onboarded them, it’s time to get started. While you may be ready to start coding right away, your new hire will need some time to adjust. As such, give them at least two weeks for proper onboarding—and then schedule regular check-ins with them during that period.

Schedule an hour once per week or every two weeks and use it as a chance for them to ask questions about company procedures, review their goals for that week, etc. Moreover, make sure you discuss how they are progressing towards their expectations within your organisation and offer assistance whenever needed.

Review project specs together; asking your new hire what questions they have about specifications can give you an idea of where confusion might arise within your workflow.

Get Exposure to Trending Technologies Using Python

For example, if you want your startup to stay current and relevant in today’s market, it’s critical that your tech team be up-to-date on important, innovative trends.

What better way to ensure your startup is on top of its game than by using cutting-edge technology like python? Python is ideal for startups looking for speed and efficiency in their development cycles. As an open source programming language , there are many easy ways for new developers to get started with python—and gain valuable experience working on your company’s next big app! If you have any questions about whether hiring python developers is right for you or what process would work best for a growing tech team, we’d love to help.

JetBrains TeamCity - May 10

Items.Title: 纯函数与非纯函数 | Pure vs Impure Functions
Items.Description:
这是您在编程语言中经常听到的两个术语，称为纯函数和不纯函数。

你知道Pure Function总是依赖于参数，不应该有任何副作用。

黑客是什么副作用当您尝试在函数中使用外部代码或更改变量时，它会产生副作用。

例子：

 let num = 10 function mul ( val ) { return num += val }

正如您在上面的代码片段中看到的， mul函数依赖于名为num的外部变量。 此外，改变num值，这使得它的功能不纯。

让我们再看一个简单的例子：

 function hello () { console . log ( " Hello Folks! " ); }

那么，您如何看待上述片段？ 是的！ 众所周知这里使用控制台，它是一个 Web API，使其功能不纯。

让我们通过示例检查其他副作用：

  1. 在纯函数中更新或修改全局变量

    let x = 10 function mul ( a , b ) { document . write ( " Started Multiplication " ) return a * b * x } console . log ( mul ( 2 , 5 ))

  2. 在这里，也改变了依赖于外部变量的外部变量。

     let x function mul ( a , b ) { x = 10 return a * b * x } console . log ( mul ( 2 , 5 ))

让我们了解 Pure 和 Impure 函数，因为现在您已经了解了副作用

纯函数

  • 如果传递相同的参数，它总是返回相同的结果
  • 它从不依赖于程序执行期间的任何状态/数据/更改
  • 它总是返回一些东西
  • 在这里，编写测试用例将是直截了当的

不纯函数

  • 更改已传递的任何参数的内部状态
  • 可能不返回任何内容即可生效
  • 编写测试用例会有点复杂，因为可能会有副作用

纯和不纯的方法

这些是纯粹的方法：

  • 数组.map()
  • 数组.reduce()
  • 数组.concat()
  • 这些是不纯的方法：

    • 数组.sort()
    • 日期.now()
    • 数学随机（）

    奖励积分🔖

    您可以将外部状态克隆为纯函数

    将外部状态克隆为纯函数不会使函数不纯。

    例子：

     let name = [ " suprabha " , " supi " ] function fullName ( newName , name ) { let clonedName = [... name ] clonedName [ clonedName . length ] = newName return clonedName } console . log ( fullName ( " sumi " , name )) // ['suprabha', 'supi', 'sumi']

    在上面的代码片段中，当您在fullName函数中使用...扩展运算符时。 它将创建它的克隆然后更新它。 因此，它不依赖于name变量。

    感谢您阅读帖子！

    希望你发现这篇文章有用。 如果您有任何问题，请随时将其放入评论部分或在这里与我联系👇

    🌟 Twitter 🌟推特📚 Ebooks 📚电子书🌟 Instagram 🌟Instagram


原文:

These are the two terms which you always hear in programming language called Pure and Impure functions.

You knows Pure Function is always dependent on arguments and there should not be any side effects.

What the hack is Side Effects? ☠️

When you try to use external code in the function or if you are mutating a variable then it create a Side Effect.

Example:

let num = 10
function mul(val) { return num += val }

As you can see in the above snippet, mul function has dependency on outer variable called num. Also, mutating num value, which makes its impure function.

Let’s checkout one more quick example:

function hello() { console.log("Hello Folks!");
}

So, what do you think about the above snippet? 🤔

.

.

.

Yeah! this, is also an impure function 😵‍💫

As you all knows that JavaScript is synchronous, using `console` or any `callback` or `promise/fetch` will make the function asynchronous.

Here using console, which is a Web API makes it impure function.

Let's checkout other side effects with examples:

  1. DOM Manipulation, any callback or reading/writing files

    function mul(a,b) { document.write("Started Multiplication") return a * b
}
console.log(mul(2,5))

  2. Updating or Modifying a global variable in pure function

    let x = 10 function mul(a,b) { document.write("Started Multiplication") return a * b * x
}
console.log(mul(2,5))

  3. Here also, mutating outer variable which is depending on an external variable.

    let x function mul(a,b) { x = 10 return a * b * x
}
console.log(mul(2,5))

Let's Understand Pure and Impure function, as now you have idea of side effects

Pure Function

  • It always returns the same result if the same arguments are passed
  • It never depend on any state/data/change during execution of a program
  • It always returns something
  • Here, writring test cases will be straight forward

Impure Function

  • Changing internal state of any argument which has been passed
  • It may take effect without returning anything
  • Writing test cases will be bit complicated as there maybe side effects

Pure and Impure Methods

These are pure methods:

  • Array.map()
  • Array.reduce()
  • Array.filter()
  • Array.concat()
  • ... (spread syntax, which mostly used to create copies)

These are impure methods:

  • Array.splice()
  • Array.sort()
  • Date.now()
  • Math.random()

Bonus Point 🔖

You can clone an external state into a pure function

Cloning an external state into a pure function does not make the function impure.

Example:

let name = ["suprabha", "supi"] function fullName(newName, name) { let clonedName = [...name] clonedName[clonedName.length] = newName return clonedName
} console.log(fullName("sumi", name)) // ['suprabha', 'supi', 'sumi']

In above snippet, as you are using ... spread operator in fullName function. It will create a clone of it and then update it. So, it doesn’t depends on name variable.

Thanks for reading the post!

Hope you found this article useful. If you have any question, feel free to drop it into comment section or reach out to me here 👇

发表于虽然我们开源的移动端是 Flutter，但我也不能决定。 我们的桌面端使用 Sciter，类似于 html/js。 我们可以在 Tauri 中使用大部分旧的 html/js。 我们也可以重用一些移动端的 Flutter 代码，但是我们的桌面设计与移动端的设计截然不同。 颤振的缺点：

  • 使用 Flutter 调试 rust 并不方便。

  • 它们都是开源的，但是 Tauri 的代码库要小得多，我们可以在 Tauri 上进行代码更改，但在 Flutter 上对我们来说很难。

我在我的 github 讨论中创建了一个池。

杰西卡时间 - May 16 5月16日

RustDesk

Posted on May 17

Though our open source mobile side is Flutter, but I can not decide either. Our desktop side uses Sciter, similar to html/js. We can use most of old html/js with Tauri. We can also reuse some mobile Flutter code, but our desktop design is super different from mobile. Flutter is not css based, few code can be shared between mobile and desktop per my understanding.

Drawback of flutter:

  • Debugging rust with Flutter is not conveninent.

  • Both of them are open source, but Tauri has much smaller code base, we can do code change on Tauri, but it is hard for us on Flutter.

I created a pool on my github discussions.
